title: Centre for Music, Film & Creative Futures
description: Explore the Center for Music, Film and Creative Futures at WPU Goa, fostering innovation in media, storytelling, arts, and emerging creative industries.

blocks.text: Rationale

India is one of the world’s most culturally vibrant nations, with creative traditions spanning classical arts, folk practices, contemporary cinema, digital media, and emerging interactive forms. As the global creative economy rapidly expands and converges with technology, there is a need for an institutional ecosystem that can bridge India’s artistic depth with future-ready creative innovation.

This Centre addresses that need by fostering interdisciplinary learning, production, and experimentation across music, film, media, storytelling, and creative technologies preparing students for impactful careers in India’s cultural economy and globally connected creative industries.
Objectives
Develop future-ready creators through interdisciplinary artistic training rooted in India’s cultural strengths.
Integrate music, film, technology, and storytelling for creative experimentation and innovation.
Offer live production, performance labs, and collaborative studio environments.- Build strong national and international industry partnerships for learning and employability.
Strengthen India’s creative footprint by promoting culturally grounded contemporary expression.
Mandate

To nurture next-generation creative excellence by integrating arts, culture, and technology; to advance India’s creative legacy through contemporary innovation; and to build globally relevant creators, storytellers, and industry contributors.
Activities
Interdisciplinary creative labs in music, film, and digital media. Built for experimentation and cross-disciplinary collaboration.
Workshops, masterclasses, and artist residencies. Offering mentorship and industry-led learning.
Production studios for film, sound, and immersive media. Supporting professional production and hands-on training.
Student showcases, festivals, and collaborative performances. Enabling visibility and creative exchange.
National and global partnerships with creative, academic, and industry bodies. Expanding access to global networks and opportunities.
Operational Plan
Annual creative festivals, film labs, and music showcases
Visiting artist and creator-in-residence programmes
Interdisciplinary project studios blending arts, science, and technology
Short-term certificate programmes and professional workshops
Student fellowships, internships, and collaborative industry projects
Partnerships and Alliances
Creative industry associations (film, music, design, media)
Production houses, studios, OTT platforms, and cultural organizations
National and global universities with strong creative-arts programs
Technology partners in XR, AI, sound engineering, animation, and interactive media
Outcomes and Impact
Creators who combine India’s cultural richness with global creative competencies. Positioned to produce work that is rooted in heritage while meeting international creative standards.
A dynamic ecosystem for interdisciplinary arts innovation. Where diverse disciplines intersect to enable experimentation, collaboration, and new forms of expression.
Strengthened industry linkages and employability pathways. Through sustained engagement with creative industries, professional networks, and real-world projects.
Increased representation of Indian creative talent on global platforms Enabling wider visibility, recognition, and participation in international creative forums.
Enhanced cultural entrepreneurship contributing to India’s creative economy. Supporting sustainable ventures, innovation, and long-term economic and cultural impact.
Be a Part of WPU GŌA
Members
Pandit Niladri Kumaar

Indian classical sitar player, composer, and the inventor of the electric sitar known as the "Zitar" Pandit Niladri Kumaar is one of India's most celebrated sitar maestros, composer, and the inventor of the groundbreaking electric sitar, the "Zitar." A fifth-generation sitar player, he is trained under his father, the legendary Pandit Kartick Kumar, a disciple of Pandit Ravi Shankar, and gave his first public performance at the age of six. He reduced the sitar's strings from 20 to five and added an electric pick-up, creating the Zitar, which bridged Indian classical music with contemporary global sounds. He has collaborated with Ustad Zakir Hussain, A.R. Rahman, and John McLaughlin, and was conferred the Sangeet Natak Akademi's Ustad Bismillah Khan Yuva Puraskar in 2007. He has produced over 15 albums and composed chartbuster Bollywood scores.
Pandit Rakesh Chaurasia

World-renowned Indian Hindustani classical musician and eminent Bansuri (flute) maestro Pandit Rakesh Chaurasia is one of India's most acclaimed Bansuri (bamboo flute) maestros and a foremost exponent of Hindustani classical music. Born on 10 January 1971 in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, he is the nephew and most accomplished disciple of the legendary flute maestro Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia, under whose guidance he began playing the Bansuri at the age of five. His enchanting style is deeply informed by vocal technique, and he draws inspiration from nature and the spiritual dimensions of music. He has collaborated with world-renowned musicians including Ustad Zakir Hussain, Béla Fleck, and Edgar Meyer, and his contribution to the Grammy-winning album As We Speak earned him two Grammy Awards in 2024.
Pandita Kaushiki Chakraborty

Indian classical vocalist of the Patiala Gharana and a prominent Hindustani classical musician Pandita Kaushiki Chakraborty is one of the most celebrated voices in Hindustani classical music and a foremost torchbearer of the Patiala Gharana. Born on 24 October 1980 in Kolkata, she is the daughter of Padma Bhushan recipient and legendary vocalist Pandit Ajoy Chakraborty, under whose guidance she trained at the prestigious ITC Sangeet Research Academy. She has become one of the first female singers from the Patiala tradition to receive international recognition, known for the gharana's elaborate melodic ornamentation and deep emotional expression. Her repertoire spans Khayals, Thumris, Dadras, and Bhajans, and she is the recipient of the BBC Radio 3 Award for World Music in the Asia-Pacific category in 2005. She also leads Sakhi, an ensemble celebrating India's diverse musical traditions through a feminine lens.
Pandit Mahesh Kale

Indian American classical vocalist and playback singer renowned for his specialization in Indian Classical (Hindustani), Semi-Classical, Devotional music including Natya Sangeet Pandit Mahesh Kale is an Indian American classical vocalist and playback singer based in the San Francisco Bay Area, renowned for his mastery of Hindustani Classical, Semi-Classical, Devotional music, and Natya Sangeet. Born on 12 January 1976 in Pune, he is a disciple of the legendary Pandit Jitendra Abhisheki of the Agra Gharana, under whose gurukul-like tutelage he trained for over eight years in classical and semi-classical forms including thumri, dadra, tappa, bhajans, and Marathi Natya Sangeet. He won India's 63rd National Film Award as Best Playback Singer for the song Aruni Kirani in the film Katyar Kaljat Ghusli. He has received a proclamation from the U.S. House of Representatives for successfully bridging the gap between classical and non-classical audiences globally.
Pandit Satyajit Talwalkar

Distinguished Indian tabla player and musician Pandit Satyajit Talwalkar is a distinguished Indian tabla artist and one of the most gifted percussionists of his generation. Son and disciple of the legendary Padmashree Pandit Suresh Talwalkar, he began learning the tabla at the age of four and gave his first public performance at nine, also inheriting musical sensibility from his mother, renowned vocalist Padma Talwalkar. His performances — whether solo or in accompaniment — are marked by catchy tempos, unanticipated crescendos, and compositions embellished with dexterous agility, his bass from the bayan and hummingbird-like action on the dayan creating a distinctive melodic flow. He has performed alongside luminaries including Niladri Kumar and Rakesh Chaurasia at prestigious national and international festivals.
Siddharth Kak

Renowned Indian documentary filmmaker, television producer, and presenter Siddharth Kak is one of India's most celebrated documentary filmmakers, television producers, and presenters. Born into a Kashmiri Pandit family, he was educated at Lawrence School, Sanawar and graduated from St. Stephen's College, Delhi. He is best known as the creator, producer, and co-host of the iconic Doordarshan series Surabhi (1990–2001), which explored India's heritage, arts, and traditions, and founded Cinema Vision India, under which he produced over 100 documentaries and multiple television series, earning three National Film Awards. He is the Founder Trustee of the Surabhi Foundation for Research and Cultural Exchange (Surabhi FORCE), developing projects of national cultural importance, and has served as Chairperson of the National Short Feature Film Awards.
Vinta Nanda

Co-Founder & Executive Director, Waterfront Indie Film Festival (WIFF) Mumbai Vinta Nanda is a Mumbai-based filmmaker, writer, content strategist, and Co-Founder & Executive Director of the Waterfront Indie Film Festival (WIFF), Mumbai. Co-conceived alongside cultural visionaries Deepa Gahlot and Shantanu Ray Chaudhuri, WIFF is an annual celebration of independent cinema, showcasing feature films, short films, documentaries, and experimental works from global and South Asian filmmakers. She previously served as Director Ideation at Zee Network, co-produced and directed the feature film White Noise starring Rahul Bose and Koel Purie, curated the Kala Ghoda Festival's film section, and co-founded the Asian Centre for Entertainment Education. She is also the founder and editor of The Daily Eye, a platform devoted to cinema, culture, and creative storytelling.
Rohini Ramnathan

Co-Founder & Executive Director, Waterfront Indie Film Festival (WIFF) Mumbai Rohini Ramnathan is the Chief Content Officer of the Fever Network (HT Media Group), overseeing content across Fever FM, Nasha, and Punjabi Fever, and also serves as Creative Director of JustTooFilmy. An award-winning radio presenter and content strategist, she was the first voice on Fever FM when it launched in 2006 — marking the beginning of a nearly two-decade journey at HT Media. In her expanded role, she leads the network's multi-platform content strategy, accelerates digital-first growth, and oversees video-led storytelling. Her elevation is a reflection of the network's strategic investment in audio and digital leadership at a pivotal moment in the evolution of India's content and entertainment landscape.
Dr. Soumya Swaminathan

Principal Advisor, Union Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for the National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme Dr. Soumya Swaminathan is the Principal Advisor to the Union Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for the National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme (NTEP), serving on a pro bono basis. A paediatrician and globally recognised expert in tuberculosis and HIV research, she was the first Chief Scientist of the World Health Organization (WHO) and previously served as Director General of the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR). With over 40 years of experience in clinical care and research, she has dedicated her career to translating vital research into impactful health programmes. In her current role, she provides technical advice on programme strategy, policy directions, and research, and assists in forming expert groups with top global talent. She also chairs the M.S. Swaminathan Research Foundation (MSSRF).
Dr. Renu Swarup

Former Secretary to Government of India, Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology Dr. Renu Swarup is a distinguished Indian geneticist and former Secretary to the Government of India, heading the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Ministry of Science & Technology. She worked in the Department of Biotechnology for over 30 years and also served as Chairperson of the Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), becoming the first woman to hold additional charge of Secretary, Department of Science & Technology. She holds a PhD in Genetics and Plant Engineering and completed post-doctoral studies at the John Innes Centre, Norwich, UK. Under her leadership at BIRAC, over 60 incubators were created and 5,000 entrepreneurs emerged, significantly advancing India's biotech ecosystem. She is a Fellow of the National Academy of Sciences, India, and a newly discovered microbial species has been named in her honour.
Dr. Sanjay Deshmukh

Director – Surgical Oncologist, Ruby Hall Clinic, Pune Dr. Sanjay Deshmukh is Director and Surgical Oncologist at Ruby Hall Clinic, Pune, with over 37 years of expertise in cancer surgery. He completed his post-graduation in General Surgery from Government Medical College, Aurangabad, followed by a four-year surgical residency and super-speciality training in Surgical Oncology at Tata Memorial Hospital, Mumbai, and a UICC Fellowship at the University of Maryland, USA in 1995. He is credited with starting Pune's first Breast Centre in 2004 and performing the first Sentinel Node Biopsy for breast cancer in Maharashtra. A founder member and West Zone Director of the Association of Breast Surgeons of India, he is also founder president of the Oncology Group of Pune, with multiple publications in national and international journals.
Mudit Dandwate

CEO & Co-Founder, Dozee Mudit Dandwate is the CEO and Co-Founder of Dozee, India's pioneering health-tech startup transforming patient care through contactless remote monitoring. A mechanical engineer, he graduated from the Indian Institute of Technology, Mumbai in 2013, and prior to founding Dozee, worked as a vehicle dynamics expert at Altair Technologies, where he designed, built, and raced four full-scale race cars, including India's first electric race cars. Dozee is a global pioneer in contactless Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Early Warning Systems, transforming any hospital bed into a smart monitoring bed through a Health-AI platform that delivers continuous, automated vital signs monitoring. His vision is to shift healthcare from reactive to proactive, making intelligent patient care accessible across every clinical setting.
Dr. Shyam Vasudeva Rao

Founder & Director, Forus Health, Renalyx and Rx DHP Dr. Shyam Vasudeva Rao is Founder and Director of Forus Health, Renalyx Health Systems, and Rx DHP — three ventures driving affordable and accessible healthcare innovation. He holds a doctorate in Real Time Embedded Systems, specialising in Parallel Computer Architecture from the Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru, with a gold medal, and has previously worked with Ericsson, Tata Consultancy Services, and as Director of Technology at Philips Innovation Campus. His innovation 3nethra, an all-in-one eye screening device developed at Forus Health, has won multiple prestigious awards including the DST Lockheed Martin Gold Medal, NASSCOM Innovation Award, and the Anjani Mashelkar Inclusive Innovation Award. He has filed over 20 international patents, groomed more than 50 research programmes in healthcare and life sciences, and serves as Technical Director for Maastricht University Medical Center, Netherlands.

title: Centre of BioHappiness
description: Learn about the Center of BioHappiness at WPU Goa, promoting wellbeing, sustainability, and human flourishing through science, research, and holistic practices.

blocks.text: Prof. M. S. Swaminathan
Prof. M. S. Swaminathan (1925–2023), revered as the Father of India’s Green Revolution, was a pioneering geneticist whose work transformed India from food scarcity to self-sufficiency.

He led premier institutions such as the Indian Agricultural Research Institute and the International Rice Research Institute, and chaired the National Commission on Farmers, championing policies that uplifted smallholder farmers. As founder of the M. S. Swaminathan Research Foundation, he advanced sustainable agriculture and rural development with a strong focus on science serving society.

A global icon of agricultural innovation, he was honoured with the Bharat Ratna, Padma Vibhushan, World Food Prize, Ramon Magsaysay Award, and many other international accolades. His legacy continues to inspire a future where science, sustainability, and social equity move hand in hand.
What We Do at the Centre
We work across crop genetics, crop improvement, climate-smart practices, and conservation of traditional seeds and agricultural biodiversity.
We enable farmer training, entrepreneurship development, and community-led innovation that keeps farmers at the heart of decision-making.
We contribute to agricultural policy, promote inclusive rural development, and foster systems that uplift smallholder and marginalized farming communities.
How You Engage in the Centre of Excellence
Undergraduate and postgraduate degree pathways
Field immersion modules
Global exchange programmes
Community labs and experiential learning
Joint certifications with international partners
Suggestive Programmes
Biodiversity & Sustainable Food Systems
Nutrition, Public Health & Well-Being
Climate Resilience & Ecological Security
Livelihoods, Equity & Community Empowerment
Technology, AI & Digital Field Labs
Policy, Leadership & Global Diplomacy
Be a Part of WPU GŌA

title: Centre of excellence for Design Research
description: Explore the Center of Excellence for Design Research at WPU Goa, driving innovation through design thinking, research, and real-world problem solving.

blocks.text: Shri Rajeev Sethi
Shri. Rajeev Sethi

Shri. Rajeev Sethi is one of South Asia’s foremost designers, scenographers, and cultural visionaries, celebrated for elevating India’s craft heritage on the global stage. Founder of the Asian Heritage Foundation and a founding member of INTACH, he has led landmark exhibitions like Aditi and The Golden Eye, and created the iconic Jaya He GVK New Museum at Mumbai Airport (T2).

His work bridges design, heritage, and community empowerment, earning him the Padma Bhushan, Order of Merit of the Federal Republic of Germany, and global recognition as a pioneer in cultural conservation and creative industries.
Rationale

In a world shaped by climate fragility, rapid technological evolution, and complex socio-cultural transitions, design research must evolve from craft-oriented problem-solving to systemic, transdisciplinary co-creation. This centre aims to:
Build design research capacity aligned with societal challenges
Provide methodological depth for the Research Institute (RI)-linked capstone structure
Support the Living Learning Scholar Community (LLSC) mission of collaborative knowledge creation
Ensure WPU GŌA’s design education meets global standards of research rigour
Objectives
Advance rigorous design methods grounded in systemic and transdisciplinary inquiry.
Develop design research that enables ethical, sustainable societal transformation.
Strengthen RI-linked research and foster high-impact capstone outcomes.
Support LLSC initiatives through community-engaged design practices.
Build global design research leadership for emerging futures.
Mandate

In a world shaped by climate fragility, rapid technological evolution, and complex socio-cultural transitions, design research must evolve from craft-oriented problem-solving to systemic, transdisciplinary co-creation. MIT-WPU GŌA governance framework emphasises integrative learning across RIs (vertical integration), LLSCs (horizontal integration), and CoEs (global validation). CEDR is required at this moment to:
provide methodological depth for the RI-linked capstone structure
support the LLSC mission of collaborative knowledge creation
ensure WPU GŌA's design education meets global standards of research rigour. MIT WPU Transdisciplinary Appro…

To develop and disseminate rigorous, transdisciplinary design research that integrates systemic thinking, human-centred inquiry, and real-world application, enabling solutions that address societal, ecological, and technological challenges across scales.
Focused Activities
Research programmes in systemic design, socio-technical futures, and institutional innovation.
Design research studios embedded in major societal challenges defined by RIs.
Teaching Support and Pedagogy Development including design theory modules, research methods, and capstone scaffolding.
Field Immersions and Community Engagement in partnership with LLSCs and local ecosystems.
Method Development and Knowledge Production (frameworks, tools, taxonomies, templates).
Collaborative Projects with global design research networks and industry partners.
Operational Plan
Annual Design Research Summit gathering international scholars and practitioners
Methods and Futures Winter/Summer Schools for students and faculty
Systemic design studios co-supervised with RI faculty for capstone alignment
Workshops and masterclasses on research methods, prototyping, design ethics, and scaling innovation
Research Publications: working papers, toolkits, design frameworks, evidence briefs
Cross-campus design challenges integrating LLSCs and transdisciplinary electives
Visiting scholar and practitioner residencies
Partnerships and Alliances
Academic
Global design research schools, centres for systemic design, speculative design, sustainability, human-computer interaction, and public systems.
Industry
Technology companies, design consultancies, regenerative design firms, mobility and product design industries, healthcare design organisations.
Government
Urban development departments, innovation missions, public service delivery organisations, sustainability and climate agencies.
Community and Civil Society

Local groups linked with LLSCs, along with NGOs and foundations working in environment, culture, public health, livelihoods, and youth development. These partnerships enable vertical integration with RIs and horizontal integration with LLSCs, aligned with the institution’s mandate.
Outcomes and Impact
Establishment of a nationally recognised hub for transdisciplinary design research
Strengthened RI-based capstone outputs through robust design methodology support
Creation of a design research ecosystem integrating technology, society, ecology, and culture
Enhanced student employability through rigorous, portfolio-quality research immersion
Global partnerships establishing WPU GŌA leadership in systemic design education
Evidence-based frameworks influencing policy, public systems, and sustainable development
Professionalisation of design research in India through methods, toolkits, and open knowledge systems
Members
Prof. Delfina Fantini van Ditmar

Professor in Research & Chair, Regenerative Art, Design (RAD) group, Royal College of Art (RCA) Prof. Delfina Fantini van Ditmar is a Professor in Research and Chair of the Regenerative Art and Design (RAD) group at the Royal College of Art (RCA), London. A biologist and design researcher, her work engages the regenerative paradigm through transdisciplinary practice, exploring ecological thinking, reflective practices, epistemological paradigms, and alternative futures. She holds a BA in Biology and completed her PhD at the Royal College of Art with a thesis entitled 'The IdIoT.' She co-directs the UKRI-funded Becoming Regenerative Lab. Her critical practice examines material ethics of care and the paradigm shifts required in design, spanning fashion, product design, and architecture. She also co-founded the Design Research Society's Special Interest Group on Design and Ethics.
Prof. Praveen Nahar

Professor of Design, IIT Gandhinagar Former Director, NID Ahmedabad Prof. Praveen Nahar is a Professor of Design at IIT Gandhinagar and former Director of the National Institute of Design (NID), Ahmedabad. He holds a Master's in Industrial Design from IIT Delhi and pursued a postgraduate course in Sustainable Design from Delft University of Technology, Netherlands, and has over 23 years of experience in design teaching, consultancy, and research. As Director of NID from 2019 to 2024, he also served as Member Secretary of the India Design Council and Chairperson of the National Design Business Incubator. His notable contributions include the design of Mumbai Suburban Trains and Mumbai Monorail, which led to the establishment of the Railway Design Centre at NID. His academic interests span systems thinking, sustainable design, social design, and strategic design.
Prof. Singanapalli Balaram

Distinguished Visiting Professor, Shiv Nadar University Founding Dean, The Aram Center for Arts, Design, and Environmental Studies (ACADES) Prof. Singanapalli Balaram is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Shiv Nadar University and the Founding Dean of the Aram Center for Arts, Design, and Environmental Studies (ACADES). A pioneering industrial designer and design educator with over five decades of experience, he is one among the founding group of design teachers who started design education in India, and is respected globally as a design educator, practitioner, author, and TEDx speaker. He pursued doctoral studies at the Royal College of Art, England, and previously served as faculty and Chairman of Education at the National Institute of Design, Ahmedabad, for over three decades. He co-founded the Society of Industrial Designers of India (SIDI) in 1980, and his research interests span universal design, design thinking, design ethics, and storytelling.
Prof. Sanjay Balkrishna Maggirwar
Prof. Rabah Boukherroub

Research Director, CNRS, and Professor, Institute of Electronics, Microelectronics & Nanotechnology, University of Lille, France Prof. Rabah Boukherroub is a Research Director at CNRS and Group Leader at the Institute of Electronics, Microelectronics and Nanotechnology (IEMN), University of Lille, France. He received his PhD in Chemistry from the University of Paul Sabatier, Toulouse, and his research interests span nanostructured functional materials, surface chemistry, and photophysics of semiconductor and metal nanostructures, with emphasis on biosensors, nanomedicine, photocatalysis, and energy-related applications. He is a co-author of over 440 research publications and 31 book chapters on nanotechnology, materials chemistry, and biosensors, and holds 11 patents. He serves as Associate Editor of ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces and as Co-editor in Chief of AI & Materials. His global collaborations span China, India, the Netherlands, and beyond.
Be a Part of WPU GŌA

title: Centre of excellence for Government Leadership And Public Policy
description: Discover the Center for Government Leadership and Public Policy at WPU Goa, shaping future leaders through policy research, governance, and public impact initiatives.

blocks.text: The T. N. Seshan Centre of Excellence at WPU GŌA is envisioned as a national platform to nurture ethical, capable, and future-ready public leaders. Rooted in Shri T. N. Seshan’s legacy of administrative courage and constitutional integrity, it builds on nearly two decades of MIT-SOG’s leadership and governance work. Responding to India’s rapidly evolving governance landscape, the Centre will offer world-class leadership development, evidence-based policymaking, and public-sector innovation. Located at WPU GŌA, it integrates governance studies with civilizational wisdom, modern pedagogy, and experiential learning.
Shri T. N. Seshan
Key Pillars and Programme Areas
Leadership for a Developed Bharat@2047
Training programmes in public leadership, administrative excellence, statesmanship, and strategic governance to prepare leaders for India’s transition into a developed nation by the year 2047.
Civilizational Wisdom and Spiritual Core in Public Leadership
Curriculum integrating inner leadership ethics, self-awareness, emotional balance, and value-based decision making rooted in Bharat’s civilizational thought.
Youth, Citizenship and Democratic Participation
Initiatives to nurture informed, responsible, and participatory young citizens equipped to engage with democratic processes.
Global Partnerships and India’s Soft Power
Collaborations that strengthen India’s global governance discourse, enabling cross-cultural learning and international policy exposure.
Bharat’s Development Pathways
Research and academic pathways focusing on India’s socio-economic transitions, institutional reforms, federal dynamics, geopolitics, and sustainability challenges.
Activities
Leadership Development and Training Programmes
Certificate and diploma courses for emerging leaders, civil society actors, and young political workers.
Executive programmes for government officials and Panchayati Raj representatives
Workshops on crisis management, ethical leadership, policy planning, negotiation, and communication.
Innovation, Technology and Public Policy Lab
Governance and Data Lab for policy simulation, digital governance, behavioural insights
AI-enabled tools for electoral analysis, service delivery, and administrative decision-making
Ethics and Public Leadership Retreats
Residential retreats with meditation, discourses on dharma and leadership, and value-based governance dialogues.
National Development Case Competitions
Student solutions to real challenges: climate resilience, urban mobility, health governance, education reform.
Partnership and Collaboration Unit
Dedicated cell for forging national and international institutional collaborations.
Democratic Education and Public Outreach
Annual T. N. Seshan Lecture Series on Democracy and Governance
Programmes with Election Commission, NGOs, and global democracy bodies
Academic Integration

The Centre will integrate its learning philosophy across university programmes through Below Programmes where Students from political science, public policy, management, law, media, AI, data science, economics, social sciences, and peace studies will collaborate on governance challenges guided by an interdisciplinary framework.
Undergraduate and postgraduate pathways
Public policy specializations
Field immersions and governance internships
International Summer Schools
Joint certificate programmes
Leadership studios and policy labs
Intended Partnerships and Alliances
Outcomes and Impact
A nationally recognized hub for leadership education and governance reform
Strengthened linkages between academia, government, and civil society
High-impact governance innovation pilots in Goa and beyond
Research outputs: white papers, policy briefs, governance toolkits
Youth-led democratic engagement and leadership pipelines
Ethical, skilled leaders prepared for public institutions and development sectors
Members
Dr. Sanjeev Chopra

IAS officer (Retd.) Former Director, Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration (LBSNAA) Dr. Sanjeev Chopra is a retired IAS officer of the 1985 batch (West Bengal cadre) and former Director of the Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration (LBSNAA), India's premier civil services training institution. Over a distinguished 36-year career, he served in multiple capacities across central and state governments, and has held the Hubert H. Humphrey Fellowship at Cornell and the Robert S. McNamara Fellowship at the World Bank, as well as positions at the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland and Harvard's Lakshmi Mittal and Family South Asia Institute. He has written extensively on public policy, developmental issues, agriculture, rural economy, literature, and culture. His authored works include We, the People of the States of Bharat (2022) and The Great Conciliator: Lal Bahadur Shastri and the Transformation of India (2025), published by Bloomsbury. He is also Patron and Honorary Curator of the Valley of Words International Literature and Arts Festival, Dehradun.
Shri Anil Swarup

IAS officer (Retd.) Founder Chairman, Nexus of Good Former Secretary, School Education & Literacy, Govt. of India Former Secretary, Ministry of Coal, Govt. of India Shri Anil Swarup is a retired IAS officer of the 1985 batch (Uttar Pradesh cadre) and one of India's most respected civil servants, known for his integrity and reform-driven leadership. A recipient of the Director's Gold Medal for best officer trainee at LBSNAA, he served in key roles across central and state governments, including as Coal Secretary and Secretary for School Education & Literacy. As Coal Secretary, he conducted transparent e-auctions of coal blocks following the Supreme Court's landmark cancellation of prior allocations, generating revenues of over ₹3 lakh crore for state governments. As Education Secretary, he fostered public-private partnerships to improve the quality of school education. He is the author of three books — No More a Civil Servant, Ethical Dilemmas of a Civil Servant, and Not Just a Civil Servant — and is Founder Chairman of the Nexus of Good Foundation.
Lt. Gen. A K Singh

Former Lt. Governor of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands, and Puducherry Former General Officer Commanding-in-Chief, Southern Command, Indian Army Lt. Gen. Ajay Kumar Singh (Retd.), PVSM, AVSM, SM, VSM, is a distinguished veteran of the Indian Army and former Lieutenant Governor of Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Puducherry. An alumnus of Sainik School Rewa and the National Defence Academy, he served as General Officer Commanding-in-Chief of the Indian Army's Southern Command before being appointed the 11th Lieutenant Governor of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in July 2013, additionally holding charge of Puducherry from July 2014 to May 2016. He was commissioned into one of India's oldest regiments, the 7th Light Cavalry, on 17 June 1973. As Lieutenant Governor, he is credited with establishing Andaman's first medical college, ANIIMS, and the second degree college of South Andaman, ANCOL, leaving a lasting impact on the islands' educational and social infrastructure.
Prof. Satish Tripathi

title: Centre of Excellence for Inclusive Innovation
description: Get insights of the Center of Strategic Leadership and Organizational Governance at WPU Goa, developing future leaders through research, strategy, and governance excellence.

blocks.text: Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar and Prof. Anil Gupta
Rationale

WPU GŌA’s transdisciplinary governance model requires Centres of Excellence to act as global validators and integrators within a system that includes Research Institutes (RIs) and Living Learning Scholar Communities (LLSCs).

This centre fulfils this mandate by enabling the university to address complex societal challenges, such as livelihoods, public health, ecological resilience, youth capability, and ethical technology, through integrated, field-embedded and research-driven inclusive innovation.

It links knowledge creation (Research Institutes), community understanding (Living Learning Scholar Communities), and global best practice (Centers of Excellence) to produce scalable, community-centred solutions.
Objectives
Advance transdisciplinary research in advanced materials, manufacturing and emergent device systems.
Design and develop new materials and affordable green processing methodologies for multiple industrial and societal applications.
Foster industry-academia-policy convergence for translational impact.
Train next-generation researchers and engineers through experiential learning and hands-on training.
Build global partnerships to strengthen India’s role in advanced manufacturing and emergent device development ecosystems.
Mandate
Activities
Transdisciplinary research programmes aligned with RI themes, including climate, livelihoods, social technology, and policy.
Field immersions and community innovation labs developed in partnership with LLSCs.
Teaching and mentorship in inclusive innovation, grassroots methods, frugal engineering, and enterprise.
Grassroots innovation scouting (Shodh Yatra-inspired engagements) in collaboration with community networks.
Social enterprise incubation for youth-led and community-driven ventures.
Policy and systems design labs collaborating with government and civic organisations.
Operational Plan
Mashelkar–Gupta Annual Innovation Lecture Series
Population-Scale Impact Summit bringing together researchers, practitioners, and policymakers
Summer/winter schools on frugal innovation, livelihoods, and social systems
LLSC-embedded community challenges and problem-solving studios
RI-aligned applied research studios for capstone programmes
Grassroots immersions across Goa and India
Fellowships for innovators, youth, and community researchers
White papers and toolkits on MLM, grassroots models, and systems innovation
Partnerships and Alliances
Industry CSR divisions, inclusive-tech companies, digital public infrastructure initiatives, social-impact incubators.
Academia Institutions specialising in inclusive innovation, public policy, sustainability, systems thinking, and design research.
Government State innovation missions, rural livelihood programmes, ministries (MSME, Environment, Education), local government bodies.
Community and Civil Society SHGs, cooperatives, grassroots innovators, NGOs, and rural and tribal communities linked to LLSCs.
Global Networks Frugal innovation labs, grassroots innovation networks, social enterprise ecosystems.
Outcomes and Impact
Establishment of a national model for frugal and grassroots inclusive innovation
Creation of integrated, field-grounded, RI-aligned research outputs
Youth trained as integrated solution architects, capable of transforming systems
Strong community partnerships through LLSC structures
Public systems and policies influenced through evidence-based insights
Scaling of community-owned enterprises, cooperatives, and livelihood models
Enhanced global visibility for WPU GŌA’s transdisciplinary mission
Members
Sushil Borde

Head, Reliance Innovation Leadership Centre, Reliance Industries Ltd. Sushil Borde is the Head of the Reliance Innovation Leadership Centre at Reliance Industries Limited, India's largest private sector company. He spearheads strategic initiatives in the Chairman's office of Reliance Industries and has also led key initiatives including Mumbai Indians and was part of the founding teams of Reliance Foundation and Jio Institute. He convened the Reliance Innovation Council of Nobel Laureates and global thought leaders and holds a patent for an innovation platform. He spent seven years in South Africa leading the Advanced Manufacturing Technology Strategy, deploying public funds in technology programmes to make South Africa's manufacturing sector globally competitive. He formerly served as President of the Fab Foundation at MIT, advancing the global 'Invention as Aid' mission through Fab Labs, and is also President of the Anjani Mashelkar Foundation, driving affordable, high-tech healthcare innovations.
Dr. Shankar Venugopal
Prof. Satish Tripathi

title: Centre of Strategic Leadership and Organizational Governance
description: Explore the Center of Transdisciplinary Research and Education at WPU Goa, integrating diverse disciplines to solve complex global challenges through innovation.

blocks.text: Organisations across the world face challenges arising from rapid market shifts, technology transitions, and rising expectations of governance. Many strategic plans do not translate into outcomes because they lack execution discipline, leadership depth, and systems that ensure accountability. Prof. Charan’s work shows that high performing institutions integrate strategy with people and operations. This centre responds to the need for leadership maturity and governance capability by embedding these principles and preparing individuals to address real organisational demands with clarity and responsibility.
Prof. Ram Charan
Key Pillars and Programme Areas
Training Programmes
Training programmes in public leadership, administrative excellence, statesmanship, and strategic governance to prepare leaders for India’s transition into a developed nation by the year 2047.
Leadership and Inner leadership
A curriculum integrating inner leadership, including ethics, self-awareness, emotional balance, and value-based decision-making, rooted in Bharat’s civilisational thought.
Youth Engagement
Initiatives to nurture informed, responsible, and participatory young citizens equipped to engage with democratic processes.
Collaborations
Collaborations that strengthen India’s global governance discourse, enabling cross-cultural learning and international policy exposure.
Research Pathways
Research and academic pathways focusing on India’s socio-economic transitions, institutional reforms, federal dynamics, geopolitics, and sustainability challenges.
Activities
Leadership academies, strategic boot camps, and governance workshops.
Boardroom simulation labs and real-time organisational problem-solving modules.
Research publications, case studies, policy briefs, and industry white papers.
Strategic consulting projects, internships, and mentorship programmes.
National and international leadership dialogues, master classes, and annual conferences.
Collaborations with corporations, consulting firms, public institutions, and global universities
Operational Plan
Distinguished lecture series featuring global CEOs and governance experts.
Annual Strategic Leadership Conclave and policy roundtables.
Summer and winter schools on strategy, execution, and governance.
White papers, theme papers, and leadership research reports.
Executive fellowships, visiting scholars, and practitioner-in-residence programmes.
Study circles and leadership labs embedded in academic curricula.​
Partnerships and Alliances
Industry
Leading corporates, family businesses, governance bodies, and consulting organisations.
Academia
Global business schools, leadership institutes, strategy centres, and think tanks.
Government
Public administration bodies, regulatory agencies, and policy organisations.
Civil Society
Social enterprises, non-profits, and community leadership ecosystems.
Outcomes and Impact
A new generation of leaders capable of strategic clarity, ethical judgement, and execution excellence.
Strengthened governance maturity across public, private, and social institutions.
High-impact collaborations with industry and global leadership bodies.
Publication of influential research, case studies, and policy documents.
Enhanced visibility of WPU GŌA as a national centre for leadership excellence.
A robust talent pipeline for organisations looking for future-ready leader.
Members
Prof. Shaphali Gupta

Professor, IIM Kozhikode Prof. Shaphali Gupta is a Professor of Marketing Management at the Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode (IIMK). She holds a PhD from the Shailesh J. Mehta School of Management, IIT Bombay, a Master's in Business Management with Marketing specialisation, and a Bachelor's in Mathematics, and is a gold medallist certified trainer by the Indian Society for Training and Development, Ministry of HRD. She serves as Senior Associate Editor of the Marketing Strategy Journal by Elsevier, and as Associate Editor of the European Journal of Marketing, Journal of Business Research, and Journal of Strategic Marketing. She has been a Research Fellow at Georgia State University's J. Mack Robinson College of Business for three years, and has been invited as a visiting professor at IPAG, Paris. Her research interests span sustainability, new-age technologies, innovation for greater good, and stakeholder engagement.
Kavita Dasan

Senior Vice-President, Talent Management & Mfg. HR Head, Suzlon Group Kavita Dasan is Senior Vice President – Talent Management & Manufacturing HR Head at Suzlon Group, India's leading renewable energy solutions company. A seasoned HR professional with over 31 years of experience, she specialises in result-oriented business leadership across all facets of talent management, including large-scale ramp-ups, talent retention during mergers and acquisitions, change management, performance-driven culture building, and career progression of the millennial workforce. Prior to Suzlon, she served as Chief People Officer at ABP Network for over four years, and earlier held Vice President – HR roles at DHFL and Zee Entertainment Enterprises. An alumna of Delhi University, she specialised in Human Resources Management from IIM Ahmedabad and is also a certified Grow More Coach.
Prof. Ronald Berenbeim

Adjunct Professor, The Stern School of Business Administration, NYU Senior Fellow, The Conference Board Prof. Ronald Berenbeim is an Adjunct Professor at the NYU Stern School of Business Administration, where he has taught Professional Responsibility: Markets, Ethics and Law since 1995, and is also a Senior Fellow at The Conference Board. A graduate of Cornell University, Balliol College Oxford (Keasbey Scholarship), and Harvard Law School, he co-authored Fighting Corruption in East Asia: Solutions from the Private Sector (The World Bank, 2003) following a World Bank study he directed on private sector anticorruption practices. He is a member of the United Nations Global Compact Anticorruption Working Group and Transparency International's Steering Committee on Business Principles for Resisting Corrupt Practices, and received a Fulbright grant in 2010 to teach business ethics at the University of Cergy-Pontoise, France. He is also a recognised Top 100 Thought Leader in Trustworthy Business Behavior.
Prof. Satish Tripathi

title: Centre of Transdisciplinary Research and Education
description: Discover the Centre for Diplomacy, Human Creativity and AI at WPU Goa, blending technology, creativity, and global diplomacy for future-ready innovation.

blocks.text: Transdisciplinarity in Research and Education

Transdisciplinary research is an approach that iteratively interweaves knowledge systems, skills, methodologies, values, and fields of expertise within inclusive and innovative collaborations. It bridges academic disciplines and community perspectives, merging science and practice to develop transformative outcomes that respond to complex societal challenges in a robust and meaningful way.

However, transdisciplinarity is not merely a tool to generate optimal solutions. It is a way of living, working and thinking that democratizes knowledge-making practices and empowers diverse stakeholders to engage actively with the problems they experience.

When scientists collaborate with society as equal partners on relevant problems, the process of knowledge discovery becomes a mutual learning process for all partners involved, shifting from a top-down to a transformative approach.
Activities

To protect and further develop the mindset and attitudinal principles of transdisciplinarity at WPU GŌA and beyond through collaboration with scholars, practitioners and grassroot initiatives with the purpose of addressing contemporary complex and entangled issues in integral and empathic manner.
To support and deliberate the basic conditions are in place to help TD learning flourish at WPU GŌA.
To build capacity of faculties and researchers at WPU GŌA to deliver TD learning and research, through workshops, faculty development programmes (FDPs), lectures, and collaborative projects.
Provide annual advisory reports for WPU GŌA on ongoing organisation, research- and teaching related activities in line with the vision of TD research and learning.
To give advice on how to establish collaborative research projects with partners in India and globally.
Advise on TDR methodology for ongoing research activities at WPU GŌA.
Members
Prof. Seema Purushothaman
Ecological Economist and Development Scholar
Dr. Enid Still
Social Scientist Ph.D., University of Passau, Germany
Dr. Iago Otero Armengol
Project Officer, University of Lausanne, Switzerland
Prof. Wiwandari Handayani
Scientific Lead, Core Partner Institution, Diponegoro University, Indonesia
Prof. Barbara Regeer
Research Chair, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
Dr. Emma Emily de Wit
Independent Researcher
Dr. Bobby Zachariah
Transdisciplinary Researcher
Dr. Jenia Mukherjee
Associate Professor, IIT Kharagpur
Exploring Transdisciplinary Research TDR

Watch this video as Prof. Barbara Regeer and Dr Emma Emily de Wit discuss Transdisciplinary Research (TDR) as a new method for collaboration and innovation needed to solve complex global challenges that don’t fit into a single discipline, aiming to make knowledge and decision-making more inclusive.
Be a Part of WPU GŌA

Ecological Economist and Development Scholar Prof. Seema Purushothaman is an Ecological Economist and Professor with the School of Development at Azim Premji University, Bengaluru, also affiliated with the Ashoka Trust for Research in Ecology and the Environment (ATREE). She holds an MSc in Agricultural Economics from Kerala Agricultural University, an MSc in Environmental Economics from the University of London, and a PhD in Land Use Dynamics in Tribal Lands of the Southern Western Ghats from the Forest Research Institute, Dehradun. Her work involves empirical, conceptual and action research spanning agrarian communities in peri-urban zones, rural landscapes, and forest peripheries, and she has co-authored the book City and the Peasant: Urbanization and Agrarian Change in Southern India. With nearly 25 years of research experience across government, corporate, and non-profit organisations, her teaching interests span sustainability as an agrarian, economic, and conceptual concern.

Project Officer, University of Lausanne, Switzerland Dr. Iago Otero Armengol is a researcher and project officer at the Interdisciplinary Centre for Mountain Research (ICMR), University of Lausanne, Switzerland. He holds a PhD in Environmental Sciences from the Autonomous University of Barcelona (2010), and has previously held post-doctoral research positions at the Integrative Research Institute on Transformations of Human-Environment Systems, Humboldt University of Berlin. His research interests span biodiversity conservation, wildfire, mountain social-ecological systems, interdisciplinarity, and sustainability transformations, with a focus on how pluralist nature valuation can inform biodiversity policy and conservation. At ICMR, he has played a central coordinating role in advancing interdisciplinary research on mountain-specific challenges including climate change, land planning, and economic vulnerability. He is also a contributor to the Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services (IPBES).
Prof. Dr. Ing. Wiwandari Handayani

Scientific Lead, Core Partner Institution, Diponegoro University, Indonesia Prof. Dr. Ing. Wiwandari Handayani is a Professor and Head of the Department of Urban and Regional Planning, Faculty of Engineering, Diponegoro University (UNDIP), Semarang, Indonesia, where she also serves as Scientific Lead as a core partner institution. She holds a bachelor's degree in Urban and Regional Planning from UNDIP, master's degrees in Urban and Regional Planning from ITB and Population Studies from the Australian National University, and a doctoral degree in Regional Development Planning from the University of Stuttgart, Germany. Since 2018, she has headed the Center for Urban and Regional Resilience Research (CURE) at Diponegoro University, and has been actively engaged in urban sustainability and resilience since 2011. Her research spans urban and regional planning, disaster risk management, climate adaptation, and governance, with deep engagement in networks including GCSMUS and NUPS, and research grants from the Australian, South Korean, and US governments.
Prof. Dr. Barbara Regeer

Research Chair, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam Prof. Dr. Barbara Regeer is a Full Professor and University Research Chair in Transdisciplinary Research for Purpose-Led Transformation in Health and Environmental Sustainability at the Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit (VU) Amsterdam. Appointed to the University Research Chair in September 2023, she focuses on transdisciplinary research practices that foster system transformations in domains such as urban food systems, mental health care, climate change, and youth protection, pioneering research on "meaningful measures" that support professionals to learn, reflect, and develop agency to act. Her research interests span multi-stakeholder processes, knowledge co-creation, social change, and mutual learning across areas including sustainable food systems, integrated rural development, mental health care, child and youth care, and disability mainstreaming. She has co-authored books on knowledge co-creation for sustainable development and her work has garnered over 3,700 citations globally.

Independent Researcher Dr. Emma Emily de Wit is an independent researcher and former affiliate of the Athena Institute at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, with expertise spanning transdisciplinary research, mental health, child and youth development, and learning. Her published work includes research on participatory approaches to mental health in low-resource settings, suicide prevention in India, and WHO mh-GAP interventions with lay mental health workers in humanitarian contexts such as South Sudan. She has also examined alternative education approaches including unschooling among Indian middle-class families, and developed parenting support programmes addressing inter-generational concerns in Pune, India. Her research is characterised by a commitment to epistemic justice, inclusive knowledge co-creation, and the application of transdisciplinary methods to address complex social and mental health challenges in diverse global contexts. She has also worked as a research consultant for Save the Children in Nepal, India and Philippines.

Transdisciplinary Researcher Dr. Bobby Zachariah is a transdisciplinary researcher and practitioner affiliated with the Athena Institute for Research on Innovation and Communication at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam. His action research focuses on the application of Human Capacity Development and the SALT (Support, Appreciate, Listen & Learn, Team) methodology across diverse areas of programming, including community development, suicide prevention and survivor support, psychosocial support, community health, and organisational development. His recent work includes research on the impact of SALT-based suicide prevention peer education programmes on peer educators. Drawing on participatory and community-led approaches, his work bridges research and practice to foster inclusive development and mental wellbeing across low-resource and underserved settings, with a particular focus on India.

Associate Professor, IIT Kharagpur Dr. Jenia Mukherjee is an Associate Professor in the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences at IIT Kharagpur, with research expertise spanning environmental history, urban political ecology, transdisciplinary waters, and urban studies. She received the prestigious Carson Writing Fellowship (2018–19) from the Rachel Carson Center for Environment and Society, Munich, for completing her book Blue Infrastructures: Natural History, Political Ecology and Urban Development in India, published by Springer Nature. She has also been awarded the Salzburg Global and Nippon Foundation Fellowship, the World Social Science Fellowship on Sustainable Urbanization by UNESCO-ISSC, and the Australian Award Fellowship at Monash University, Melbourne. She is an active member of the Panta Rhei Network of the International Association of Hydrological Sciences and serves on the editorial boards of multiple international journals.

title: Centre for Diplomacy, Human Creativity and Artificial Intelligence
description: Explore the College of Human Performance and Longevity at WPU Goa, focusing on health, wellbeing, performance science, and long-term human development.

hero_image.alt: Paul-Gordon-Emerson - Centre for Diplomacy, Human Creativity and Artificial Intelligence

blocks.text: To drive international research and innovation where human creativity, cognition, and artificial intelligence converge creating human-focused AI solutions, structures, and creative approaches that strengthen cross-cultural understanding, conflict resolution, and community welfare through interdisciplinary partnerships.
Prof. Fernando Garibay

Founder and Chairman, The Garibay Institute GRAMMY® & BMI Award Winning Producer Professor, Thunderbird School of Global Management Professor, Herberger ASU Visiting lecturer at Harvard, MIT, Stanford

This centre is established to honour the transformative contributions of the Garibay Institute of Soft Power & Public Diplomacy, led by its Founder and Chairman, Prof. Fernando Garibay. The Garibay Institute has pioneered global discourse on the irreducible human spirit, creative intelligence, and the ethical integration of artificial intelligence at the intersection of art, technology, and human expression. This centre extends that institutional legacy by championing frameworks that position human imagination, emotion, and cognition at the heart of AI development, uniting artists, choreographers, technologists, cognitive scientists, and diplomats in pursuit of research that amplifies rather than replaces humanity’s collective pursuit of meaning and well-being.
Focus Areas
The Immutability of Human Creativity
Explores imagination, cognition, neuro-creativity, and non-replicable aspects of consciousness
Human-Centred Artificial Intelligence
Explores explainability, ethics, emotional AI, and flourishing beyond computation
Creative Industries and Culture-Tech
Explores XR, immersive arts, creative computing, performance technology
Public Diplomacy and Soft Power as Human Resonance
Explores empathy, cultural resonance, moral imagination in diplomacy, and art as policy
Embodied Intelligence and Movement Studies
Explores dance, choreography, kinesthetic creativity, somatic knowledge
Society, Emotion and Sense Literacy
Explores creative expression for wellbeing, collective meaning-making
Transdisciplinary Creative Systems
Explores art–science–technology convergence, uniquely human traits in automated systems
Activities
Fundamental and applied research on human creativity beyond computation, including embodied cognition.
Sponsored research projects examining AI’s relationship to consciousness, creativity, and movement.
Creative-AI labs, prototyping studios, choreographic research, and field-based studies.
Capacity building for faculty, scholars, practitioners, choreographers, and future cultural diplomats.
International collaborations with universities, studios, performing arts institutions, and AI institutes.
Residencies for artists, choreographers, dancers, designers, technologists, cultural diplomats, and creative thinkers.
Operational Plan
Annual international conference on The Irreducible Human Spirit and AI
Distinguished scholar and artist visits, research seminars, and public lectures
Think-tank series featuring cultural diplomats, choreographers, policymakers, and creative leaders
Annual global residency programme for artists, choreographers, technologists, and diplomats
Grants, sponsored projects, and multi-year research cycles
Creative showcases, choreographic works, exhibitions, and immersive technology demonstrations
Workshops uniting art, policy, and global exchange methodologies
Partnerships and Alliances
A new generation of leaders capable of strategic clarity, ethical judgement, and execution excellence.
Strengthened governance maturity across public, private, and social institutions.
High-impact collaborations with industry and global leadership bodies.
Publication of influential research, case studies, and policy documents.
Enhanced visibility of WPU GŌA as a national centre for leadership excellence.
A robust talent pipeline for organisations looking for future-ready leader.
Outcomes and Impact
New global frameworks distinguishing indispensable qualities of human contribution, including embodied knowledge.
Breakthrough research on the irreducible human spirit in an age of synthetic intelligence.
Tools and technologies integrating art, policy, and cultural exchange for education and diplomacy.
Strong international alliances elevating India's creative-tech and soft-power leadership.
Unique career pathways for students in the creative-AI economy, cultural diplomacy, and performing arts.
Enhanced understanding of how technology amplifies human flourishing across artistic disciplines.
Recognition of WPU GŌA as a hub for human-centric AI research and cultural innovation.
Members
Paul Gordon Emerson

Co-Founder, Executive & Co-Artistic Director, Company | E Paul Gordon Emerson is the Co-Founder, Executive, and Co-Artistic Director of Company | E, a Washington, DC-based performing arts organisation at the intersection of dance, culture, and diplomacy. A native New Yorker, he previously served as Legislative Director on Capitol Hill and pursued a Master's degree in Security Policy Studies at George Washington University before discovering a passion for dance, combining his foreign policy interests with culture. He has led Company | E on international cultural engagements in partnership with the U.S. Department of State across 50+ countries, and directs the company's innovations in 3D world building and the intersection of art, education, and technology, including a VR SuperMuseum in development with the State Department.
Prof. James Cooper

Professor, California Western School of Law Fellow, Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS) Prof. James Cooper is a Professor of Law and Director of International Legal Studies at California Western School of Law, San Diego, and a Research Fellow at the Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS). A Cambridge University-trained Barrister and Solicitor, he began his career with Baker & McKenzie and has held faculty positions at the University of Strathclyde, Glasgow, and the University of Toronto Faculty of Law. He has consulted for the U.S. Departments of Justice and State, the Organisation of American States, and numerous governments and international agencies across Latin America and Asia. His current research focuses on the legal regulation of emerging technologies including blockchain, artificial intelligence, and the metaverse, and he has been widely profiled in publications such as the Wall Street Journal, Time Magazine, and the New York Times.
Prof. Ronald C. Gunnell

President, The Garibay Institute Founder & Global Chairman, Truth Alone Triumphs - Global Enterprises, LLC Prof. Ronald C. Gunnell is President of the Garibay Institute for Soft Power and Public Diplomacy and Founder & Global Chairman of Truth Alone Triumphs – Global Enterprises, LLC. The Los Angeles-based Garibay Institute, founded by musical icon Fernando Garibay, engages a network spanning 192 countries and collaborates with leading universities including Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Oxford, and Cambridge. Gunnell holds joint professorial appointments with the Thunderbird School of Global Management and the Herberger Institute for Design and the Arts. His enterprise specialises in global consulting and dialogue bridge-building across the United States, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Brazil, and India. His academic interests span cross-cultural leadership, creative diplomacy, design thinking in governance, and the arts–international development nexus, with lectures delivered across continents and contributions to global policy forums.

title: College of Human Performance and Longevity
description: Explore human performance and longevity programs at WPU Goa focused on wellness, fitness, mental resilience, and holistic development.

blocks.text: The College of Human Performance and Longevity is the sixth, but distinctly vital, dimension of the entire university framework. WPU GŌA is India’s first Human Performance and Longevity Campus. While other elements of the university framework focus on the mind and purpose, this College shapes the body, discipline, lifestyle, and resilience of students.

The College of Human Performance & Longevity at WPU GŌA aims to create a future-ready generation by integrating sports science, health education, and human performance practices into everyday university life. The objective is to empower students with scientific awareness, disciplined habits, and lifelong wellness skills that support academic excellence, personal growth, and long-term health.
Beyond Intellectual Capability

Our guiding philosophy is simple: students must not only learn well, they should also live well. We believe that true success requires the synergy of a strong mind, a resilient body, a disciplined lifestyle, and a purpose-driven spirit. This College ensures students graduate with not just intellectual capability but also physical resilience, mental clarity, and lifelong wellness habits. Here we integrate sports, fitness science, lifestyle education, and world-class athletic development.
Focus Areas
Longevity Campus Initiative
WPU Goa is India’s first campus designed to extend healthy lifespan using science and habit formation. The campus design is movement-friendly, and dining is “Blue-zone inspired.”.
Sports Centre of Excellence

This provides sports resources for students. Key features include scientific training systems and coaching. This provides a real-world training ground and nurtures students for state, national, and international competitions.
Campus Health, Nutrition and Fitness Program

Health is embedded into daily life, not treated as an afterthought. This includes daily movement routines, strength and mobility training, smart nutrition guidelines for dining services, regular fitness assessments, and sleep/stress management sessions. The impact is healthier, sharper, and more energetic students with improved academic focus.
Our Core Educational Pillars
At the heart of this transdisciplinary university ecosystem lie five interconnected dimensions, each designed to nurture a transdisciplinary mindset, holistic learning, and human flourishing.
The Academic Core

Our Centre of Excellence strengthen the university’s capacity to explore relevant themes, including spirituality, agriculture, governance, leadership, and more, creating hubs of advanced knowledge and innovation.
Transdisciplinary Living Learning Societies (TLLSs)
Health and Fitness

To help students realize their full physical potential, WPU Goa offers a structured Health & Fitness Assessment Program grounded in science and guided by purpose. This one-of-its-kind initiative—exclusively designed and pioneered by WPU Goa—sets the university apart. This program enables students to understand their metabolic age in relation to their biological age, fostering evidence-based awareness of their true health and fitness status.
Baseline Screening: BMI, body composition, flexibility, and posture
Physiology Testing: Endurance, strength, speed, and agility
Performance Monitoring: Annual fitness tracking, report cards, and personalized plans
Preventive Health & Wellness
Regular medical check-ups
Injury prevention workshops conducted by experts
Sports nutrition guidance by certified professionals
Stress management, yoga, and recovery programs
Student Experience

The College of Human Performance and Longevity is a living laboratory for human performance, shaping leaders who possess the discipline, resilience, and mindset of high-performing athletes. It provides a holistic education where the mind, body, and spirit grow together.

Wellness and performance are deeply embedded into the curriculum and residential experience. Daily routines, physical literacy sessions, and community wellness activities are tied to the residential experience, supporting discipline and physical literacy. Students participate in a structured Health and Fitness Assessment Program which includes baseline fitness screening, sports physiology testing, performance monitoring, and preventive health and wellness (regular check-ups, injury prevention, and nutrition guidance).

The campus promotes an inclusive sports culture with modern multi-sport infrastructure and a goal that “Every student plays”. Sports participation enhances teamwork, leadership, discipline, and decision-making under pressure.
Impact on Students

Through guided exposure to movement science, exercise physiology, nutrition, and recovery, students bridge academic learning with real-world application. They gain confidence through self-awareness, learn how to train and recover intelligently, and build disciplined habits that support energy, focus, and balance throughout life.

This scientific and preventive approach helps every student stay fit, confident, and future-ready, promoting a lifestyle free from preventable illness—where health is not an obligation, but the foundation for lifelong success.
Be a Part of WPU GŌA
Frequently Asked Questions

title: Disclaimer
description: Read the disclaimer of WPU Goa outlining website information accuracy, usage limitations, and policies for content, links, and user responsibility.

blocks.text: WPU GŌA makes every effort to ensure that the information presented on its website, digital platforms, brochures, presentations, and other communication materials is accurate and up to date. However, all content is provided for general informational purposes only and is subject to change without prior notice.
1. Representational Nature of Content

Photographs, videos, renderings, campus visuals, event images, student life depictions, and facility illustrations displayed on the website or in promotional materials may be representative, simulated, artist’s impressions, or sourced from similar institutional activities. Actual facilities, infrastructure, equipment, environments, or experiences may vary in appearance, scale, or configuration.
2. Academic Programs & Curriculum

WPU GŌA reserves the right to modify, revise, restructure, suspend, or discontinue any academic program, course, curriculum component, credit structure, immersion plan, exchange program, internship structure, capstone format, or academic requirement at its discretion, in alignment with academic review, regulatory requirements, industry relevance, or institutional priorities.

Descriptions of learning models, experiential components, research opportunities, immersions, studios, and academic pathways represent current academic intent, not a legally binding commitment to specific formats, partners, locations, or structures.
3. Experiential Learning, Immersions & Events

All references to immersions, industry visits, trade shows, exchanges, Life Transformation Centre programs, Active Citizenship Programs, festivals, conferences, guest lectures, and external engagements are indicative in nature. WPU GŌA may change, destinations, venues, timelines, duration, partners, mode or scope or format of activities. Such changes may arise due to academic decisions, safety considerations, logistical constraints, regulatory advisories, force majeure events, geopolitical developments, institutional priorities, or availability of collaborators. Participation in certain activities may also be subject to eligibility, performance, conduct, health, visa approvals, or external approvals.

References to specific countries, cities, institutions, industries, or organizations represent academic intent and planning. They shall not be construed as binding commitments or guarantees that any particular destination, partner, or organization will form part of the student experience.
4. Faculty & Guest Engagements

References to international faculty, visiting scholars, professors of practice, industry experts, or guest mentors are based on academic planning and engagement intent. Actual participation may vary depending on availability, scheduling, regulatory permissions, or institutional needs.
5. Policies, Regulations & Institutional Rules

All university policies including but not limited to academic regulations, attendance requirements, residential policies, code of conduct, safety rules, assessment methods, fee structures, scholarships, refund policies, disciplinary procedures, wellness requirements, and campus governance are subject to revision, amendment, or replacement as determined by WPU GŌA and applicable regulatory bodies. The most current official policy documents that are available through formal university channels shall prevail over website content.
6. Fees, Financial Information & Inclusions

Fee structures, inclusions, exclusions, and financial policies are subject to institutional and regulatory review. While efforts are made to provide accurate information, the university reserves the right to make revisions in accordance with academic, operational, or statutory requirements.
7. Third-Party Associations

Mentions of industry partners, universities, institutions, or organizations represent collaborative intent or past / ongoing engagement. Such associations do not constitute legal partnerships, guarantees of placement, or permanent arrangements.
8. Career Outcomes & Opportunities

Any reference to career readiness, industry exposure, higher studies pathways, or professional opportunities reflects the university’s educational approach and support systems. These do not constitute guarantees of employment, salary, admission to other institutions, or specific career outcomes.
9. Force Majeure

WPU GŌA shall not be held liable for disruptions, modifications, delays, or cancellations of academic or experiential components arising from circumstances beyond institutional control, including but not limited to natural disasters, pandemics, public health advisories, government directives, travel restrictions, political events, or other force majeure situations.
10. Limitation of Liability

By accessing WPU GŌA’s website or materials, users acknowledge that information provided is subject to change and that the university shall not be liable for any reliance placed on such information for decision-making without formal written confirmation through official admission or institutional documents.
11. Governing Documents

In the event of any discrepancy between website content and official university regulations, admission offer letters, signed agreements, or statutory notifications, the official documents issued by WPU GŌA shall prevail.
