title: Educational Model
description: Discover WPU Goa’s educational model blending academics, real-world learning, global exposure, and transdisciplinary skills for future-ready careers.

blocks.text: At WPU GŌA, learning goes beyond classrooms to blend real world experiences. Our model blends powerful academic foundations with real-world exposure, global immersion, and human-centred development, shaping students who can think boldly, lead responsibly, and create meaningful impact.
Why Today’s World Needs a New Way of Learning

Students, whether they have studied Mechanical Engineering, Psychology or Mathematics, need to be trained comprehensively to be able to navigate and address 21st century situations, to respond to questions of innovation, technological advancement, and societal conflicts, with agility, curiosity and specific skills to collaborate across domains.

This way, at WPU GŌA you are trained to flexibly adjust to various conditions, to help steer complex decision making processes at leadership positions, and to be a unique asset in any room where new ideas and innovations are created, implemented and valorised.
Our Core Educational Pillars
At the heart of this transdisciplinary university ecosystem lie five interconnected dimensions, each designed to nurture a transdisciplinary mindset, holistic learning, and human flourishing.
The Academic Core
A rigorous foundation that interweaves knowledge domains, integrates real-world challenges, and equips students with analytical depth and conceptual clarity.
Inquiry-driven spaces that pursue deep, sustained, long-term research in thematic domains, scholarly output, publications, and funded research projects.

Our Centres of Excellence strengthen the university’s capacity to explore relevant themes, including spirituality, agriculture, governance, leadership, and more, creating hubs of advanced knowledge and innovation.
Living Learning Scholar Communities
Theme-based intellectual communities where students question, learn, and create together as they live on the campus.
College of Human Performance and Longevity
A vibrant developmental ecosystem that supports physical fitness, emotional resilience, mental clarity, and lifelong habits for high performance.
Individually Strong. Together, Transformative.
WPU GŌA is built on five interconnected pillars that work together to transform education.
Your Academic Journey
We have put effort and attention into designing the ultimate academic pathway and model for you to learn in. These include five pillars that allow for students to:
Specialise within their domain
Work and learn across domains
Apply their knowledge in settings of real-world problems.
Combine all of these lessons in their uniquely guided and immersive capstone project.
Working together with a variety of stakeholders and research institutes.
Become theoretically and practically groomed in what we call 'transdisciplinary skills'.
Academic Structure

At WPU GŌA, we are building graduates with depth in discipline, breadth across domains, and integration through design and systems thinking. You graduate as a professional with Deep expertise + broad integration + real-world experience = careers traditional education doesn’t prepare you for.
Disciplinary Programmes
Specialization
Choose 1-2 fields different from your major. Build unexpected combinations that make you irreplaceable.
University Core
Real-World Integration
All your learning is connected to real-world problems through the Research Institutes.
The World is your Classroom
A Seamless Path from Foundation to Mastery
At each stage of your educational journey at WPU GŌA you will participate in international, national, social, rural immersion Programmes designed to enrich your academic experience
Frequently Asked Questions
The Integrated Ecosystem

WPU GŌA is designed as a single, interconnected learning ecosystem rather than a collection of isolated departments. Academics, research, residential life, wellness, and industry engagement operate synchronously with each other.
The ecosystem integrates five interdependent pillars:
Academic Programmes – Transdisciplinary curricula with a strong common core
Living Learning Scholar Communities (LLSCs) – Where students live, learn, and grow together under faculty mentorship
Research Institutes (RIs) – Early exposure to real research and discovery
Centres of Excellence (CoEs) – Industry-facing application and innovation hubs
College of Human Performance & Longevity – Physical, mental, and lifestyle development

In traditional models, academics, residence, wellness, and career preparation are separate. At WPU GŌA, they reinforce each other. Classroom learning informs projects, residential communities reinforce leadership and ethics, research feeds into industry applications, and wellness sustains long-term performance.

LLSCs are small cohorts (15–20 students) that function as intellectual and social micro-communities. They integrate academic discussion, leadership development, ethical reasoning, and peer learning into daily life.

From Year 1. Research Institutes and Centres of Excellence are open to undergraduates, allowing students to participate in discovery, labs, studios, and applied problem-solving early in their academic journey.
Real-world problems do not arrive neatly labelled by discipline. An ecosystem model ensures students can:
Integrate thinking across domains
Apply theory in lived contexts
Develop leadership and ethics alongside intellect
This mirrors how professional environments actually function.
Students engage with:
Faculty research projects
Data collection and analysis
Design and experimentation
Early academic publishing or prototyping
This prepares them for higher studies and research-led careers.
CoEs are industry-facing:
Led with practitioners
Focused on real problems and outputs
Hosting studios, hackathons, and masterclasses
They translate academic insight into action.

title: WPU Goa FAQs on Admissions, Programs and Campus Life
description: Get answers to common queries about admissions, scholarships, programs, student support, and campus experiences.

blocks.text: Everything you need to know
B.Des. Communication Design

It combines visual craft with strategic thinking, research, behavioural insight, technology, and systems thinking. Students complete two specialisations (one Within-Domain and one Cross-Domain), engage in intensive studios every semester, undertake extensive immersions, andwork on real-world communication projects, preparing them not just to create visuals, but to design meaningful, effective, and responsible communication systems.

Yes. You choose one Within-Domain Specialisation and one Cross-Domain Specialisation. You can also design a custom Cross-Domain pathway under faculty guidance and select Directed Electives to further personalise your learning.
• Visual Communication & Identity Systems Design • Animation and VFX Design • Interaction Design

A four-week, credit-bearing, self-directed period after Semester 5 where students pursue an independent design research project, product development exploration, or applied design challenge. It builds design independence and often informs the final Capstone.

Highly practical. Students learn through studio-based practice, build physical and digital prototypes, use professional digital media labs, engage with industry partners, and complete real client or social impact projects throughout the four years.

Every semester includes at least one transdisciplinary studio, an intensive, project-based learning experience where you work in teams on real organisational challenges. Studios emphasise application, peer critique, iteration, and tangible deliverables (business plans, strategy decks, prototypes, etc.).

Absolutely. The Cross-Domain Specialisation options, combined with venture labs, incubation support through Centres of Excellence, and the option to convert your Capstone into a live communication or design venture, make the programme highly entrepreneurship-friendly.
Minimum 50% aggregate in Class 12/10+2 (any stream) with English as a compulsory subject. Submission of a portfolio showcasing creative work and design thinking is mandatory.
Yes. All B.Des. candidates must submit a portfolio that demonstrates their creative abilities, visual communication skills, and design thinking.
Yes. You can register with “Result Awaited” status and predicted scores. Final admission is confirmed after submission of the official marksheet.

Be authentic and reflective. Share your journey, why you want to study communication design, meaningful experiences, challenges overcome, and how your values align with WPU GŌA’s transdisciplinary and ethical philosophy. Focus on self-awareness and aspirations (max 750 words).
Yes. WPU GŌA is a fully residential campus. Living in Learning Scholar Communities (LLSCs) is an integral part of the learning experience.
₹7,50,000 per year (fixed for all four years, no escalation). This inaugural batch fee includes tuition, residential accommodation, meals, and all mandatory immersions.
Yes. Merit-based and special scholarships are offered.

You will complete approximately 11 months of structured professional experience through progressive immersions, a two-month social internship, a 12-week industry internship, and a full-semester Capstone.
Yes. They form an integral part of the curriculum and are fully covered in the annual fee.

Students undertake a two-month social internship (SOLID) with NGOs or social enterprises in Year 2, a three-month professional internship in design studios, advertising agencies, or digital media companies in Year 3, and a full-semester Capstone project in Year 4. Together, these provide structured, progressive professional experience across diverse creative sectors.

Graduates can pursue careers in branding, advertising, digital media, motion graphics, UX/UI, information design, social impact communication, publishing, content strategy, and creative direction. Many also prepare for higher studies at leading global institutions or launch their own design studios.
Yes. The strong research focus, IIP, and Capstone make graduates highly competitive for postgraduate programmes in India and abroad.

Career readiness begins in Year 1. You build a strong Digital Portfolio, gain extensive industry exposure, and complete a high-impact Capstone. The university leverages the broader MIT-WPU network and industry partnerships for internships, projects, and final placements.

WPU GŌA is a fully residential campus with Living Learning Scholar Communities (LLSCs) that enhance peer learning and personal growth. Students have access to studios, digital labs, makerspaces, sports facilities, and a vibrant creative community of peers from diverse backgrounds.
Yes. The programme is tech-enabled and requires students to bring their own laptop.
Email: admissions@wpugoa.edu.in | Helpline: +91 86050 07433
B.Des. Integrated Product Design

It combines strong design skills with engineering sensitivity, behavioural insight, business strategy, and sustainability focus. Students complete two specialisations (one Within-Domain and one Cross-Domain), engage in intensive studios every semester, undertake extensiveimmersions, and work on real-world projects, preparing them not just to design products, but to create meaningful, sustainable, and impactful solutions.
• Service & Systems Design • Sustainable Design • Interaction Design

A four-week, credit-bearing, self-directed period after Semester 5 during which students pursue an independent design research project, product development exploration, or applied design challenge. It builds design independence and often informs the final Capstone.

Highly practical. Students learn through studio-based practice, build physical and digital prototypes, use advanced fabrication labs, engage with industry partners, and complete real client or social impact projects throughout the four years.

Absolutely. The Cross-Domain Specialisation options, combined with venture labs, incubation support through Centres of Excellence, and the option to convert your Capstone into a live design venture, make the programme highly entrepreneurship-friendly.

title: Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave
description: The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave is a landmark initiative that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and society to shape a progressive and sustainable future for

blocks.text: 16 April 2026
Overview:

The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave is a landmark initiative that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and society to shape a progressive and sustainable future for Goa.

Organised by the Goa Chamber of Commerce & Industry (GCCI) in collaboration with MIT World Peace University, Goa , the conclave aims to foster meaningful dialogue, encourage collaboration, and create actionable pathways for growth that balance economic development with ecological responsibility.
Key Highlight
Gomant Gaurav Sanman
A special highlight of the conclave is the prestigious Gomant Gaurav Sanman , honouring Prof. Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar for his exceptional contributions to science, innovation, and nation-building.
The award will be presented in the august presence of Shri. Pramod Sawant , Hon’ble Chief Minister of Goa.
Conclave Themes
Reimagining Goa’s Future: Industrial Growth with Ecological Balance
Exploring how Goa can achieve sustainable industrial growth while preserving its ecological heritage through responsible policies, innovation, and collaboration.
Goa as a Global Knowledge Hub: Transdisciplinary Education & Industry-Academia Synergy
Focusing on the role of education, research, and industry partnerships in positioning Goa as a global centre for knowledge and innovation.
Distinguished Speakers & Guests
Shri. Pramod Sawant
Hon’ble Chief Minister of Goa
Prof. Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar
Chief Guest & Awardee
Dr. Rahul V. Karad
Managing Trustee & Executive President, MAEER’s MIT Group

Director, Goa Paints & Allied Products Pvt. Ltd. Industrialist & Entrepreneurial Leader

Prof. Abhishek Ranjan
Global ESG & Sustainability Leader | Chairman, ASSOCHAM Karnataka (CSR & Sustainability) Joint Secretary, UN Global Compact Network India
Expert Panalist - Session 2

National Research Professor (Ayush) | Former Vice Chairman, University Grants Commission (UGC) Chairman, Interdisciplinary AYUSH R&D Task Force on COVID-19
Dr. Bhushan V. Bhave
Principal, Vidya Prabodhini College of Commerce, Education, Computer & Management Renowned Academician, Author & Konkani Scholar

Director, BITS Pilani (K.K. Birla Goa Campus) Senior Professor, Department of Biological Sciences

Be part of a forward-looking platform that not only discusses Goa’s future but actively contributes to shaping it—through collaboration, innovation, and shared vision.
Join us in celebrating excellence and shaping Goa’s future. Register your interest to be part of this landmark conclave.

title: Goa 2037 Knowledge Industry Sustainability Conclave Event
description: Explore key takeaways and discussions from a landmark gathering focused on sustainable growth and knowledge industries.

hero_image.alt: goa 2037 knowledge industry sustainability conclave past event

blocks.text: The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave was conceptualised as a strategic platform to align Goa’s long-term development vision with the national goal of *Viksit Bharat@2047.

The conclave brought together policymakers, industry leaders, academicians, and thought leaders to deliberate on building a future-ready Goa focusing on sustainable industrial growth, innovation-driven development, and strengthening industry-academia collaboration.
Key Messages :
Hon’ble CM of Goa Dr. Pramod Sawant sharing thoughts on the establishment of WPU GŌA
Dr. Raghunath A. Mashelkar on receiving Gomant Gaurav Sanman
Mr. Mauvin Godinho, Hon Minister for Transport and Industries, Gov. of Goa
Session 1: Reimagining Goa’s Future: Industrial Growth with Ecological Balance
Mr. Pravimal Abhishek Polumatla, IAS
Secretary (Science, Technology & Waste Management), Government of Goa Managing Director, Goa Industrial Development Corporation (GIDC)
Prof. Abhishek Ranjan
Global ESG & Sustainability Leader | Chairman, ASSOCHAM Karnataka (CSR & Sustainability) Joint Secretary, UN Global Compact Network India
Mr. Anirudh Dempo
Director, Goa Paints & Allied Products Pvt. Ltd. Industrialist & Entrepreneurial Leader
Mr. Rajkumar Kamat
Promoter, EP Kamat Group Managing Director, EP Biocomposites Ltd.
Mr. Manguirish Pai Raiker
Former Chairman, National Council for MSME, ASSOCHAM Chairman & Managing Director, Brown Packaging System Pvt. Ltd.
Moderator: Mr. Sanjay Amonkar
Director General, Goa Chamber of Commerce & Industry (GCCI)

The session focused on balancing industrial growth with environmental sustainability in Goa’s limited land context. Panellists emphasized the need for adopting the “more from less for more” philosophy maximising output while minimising resource consumption.

Key discussions revolved around promoting green industries, encouraging R&D-led manufacturing, and leveraging vertical expansion over horizontal sprawl. The panel also highlighted the importance of measurable sustainability goals and policy frameworks that enable responsible industrialisation while preserving Goa’s ecological identity.
Session 2: Goa as a Global Knowledge Hub: Transdisciplinary Education & Industry-Academia Synergy
Mr. Prasad Lolayekar, IAS
Secretary (Education & Official Language), Government of Goa
Dr. Sandeep Sancheti

Vice President of Research Relations & Academic Affairs, Elsevier (India) Co-founder, Edorer.com, Former VC, SRM Institute of Science and Tech., Chenna, Former Provost-VC, Marwadi University, Rajkot, Gujarat
Dr. Bhushan Patwardhan

Former Chairman, Executive Committee, National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), National Research Professor – Ayush, Interdisciplinary School of Health Sciences, Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune
Mr. Yatin Kakodkar
Director, Apex Packing Products Pvt. Ltd, Vice President, GCCI
Dr. Bhushan V. Bhave
Principal, Vidya Prabodhini College of Commerce, Education, Computer and Management, Vidya Nagar, Alto, Parvari- Goa
Dr. Suman Kundu
Director, BITS Pilani, Goa
Moderator: Prof. Walter Leal Filho
Vice Chancellor, MIT WPU Goa, Professor, Hamburg University of Applied Sciences, Germany

This session explored how Goa can emerge as a global knowledge hub through stronger collaboration between academia and industry. Discussions highlighted the urgent need for curriculum reform, skill-based education, and experiential learning models that align with industry requirements.

Speakers stressed leveraging Goa’s unique strengths in tourism, research, and integrative health, while building a future-ready workforce. The importance of transdisciplinary education and innovation ecosystems was underscored as key drivers for global competitiveness.
Gomant Gaurav Sanman
A special highlight of the conclave is the prestigious Gomant Gaurav Sanman , honouring Prof. Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar for his exceptional contributions to science, innovation, and nation-building.
The award will be presented in the august presence of Shri. Pramod Sawant , Hon’ble Chief Minister of Goa.
Dignitaries & Speakers :
Dr. Pramod Sawant
Hon’ble Chief Minister of Goa
Mr. Mauvin Godinho
Hon’ble Minister for Transport, Industries, Panchayati Raj, Govt. of Goa
Dr. R. A. Mashelkar
FRS - Padma Vibhushan, Former DG, CSIR & Secretary to Govt of India, Eminent Scientist
Dr. Rahul V. Karad
Executive President, MIT World Peace University & Founder, MIT School of Government, BCS, NLC
Mrs. Pratima Dhond
President, Goa Chamber of Commerce & Industry (GCCI)
Mr. Sanjay Amonkar
Overview:

The highlight of the conclave was the conferment of the prestigious ‘Gomant Gaurav Sanman’ upon Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar at a grand ceremony held at Kala Academy, Goa. The award was presented by Hon’ble Chief Minister Dr. Pramod Sawant, who also announced the establishment of WPU Goa University, marking a significant milestone in the state’s educational landscape.

In his address, the Chief Minister highlighted Goa’s commitment to innovation through a strong government-industry-academia partnership. Mr. Mauvin Godinho acknowledged Dr. Mashelkar’s enduring contributions to science and society, calling it a proud moment for Goa.

In his acceptance speech, Dr. Mashelkar delivered an emotional tribute to his mother, reflecting on his humble beginnings and reaffirming his lifelong commitment to using science and innovation for the benefit of the poorest sections of society. The segment served as a powerful reminder of the impact of vision, perseverance, and purpose-driven leadership.
Media & Coverage :

title: Goa Residential Campus
description: WPU GŌA is designed as a fully residential university in India , where students do not simply attend classes and return to a hostel. They live, learn, collaborate, reflect, and grow within one connected campus

blocks.text: Live Where Learning Never Stops

WPU GŌA is designed as a fully residential university in India , where students do not simply attend classes and return to a hostel. They live, learn, collaborate, reflect, and grow within one connected campus environment.

Set in Goa, the residential campus brings together academic life, peer learning, wellbeing, sport, dining, cultural activity, and community living. Each space is designed to help facilitate a deeper university experience where learning happens outside the classroom and in everyday life.
A Fully Residential Learning Experience
Learning that continues beyond the classroom.
At WPU GŌA, residential life is not an add-on. It is central to the university’s academic model.

Students live on campus and interact daily with peers from different programmes, including engineering, design, psychology, and management. This creates a learning environment where ideas move across disciplines and students begin to understand how the real world actually works.

A conversation in class can continue in a common space. A project can become a collaboration across domains. A student can learn from faculty, mentors, peers, and campus experiences throughout the day.
This makes WPU GŌA more than a campus. It becomes a living-learning ecosystem.
Built for Transdisciplinary Learning
Where every discipline adds a new perspective.
WPU GŌA follows a transdisciplinary approach to education. It encourages students to think beyond the confines of the subject and to make connections between ideas in different areas.
The residential campus amplifies this model. When students live and learn together, collaboration becomes organic.
Engineering students can exchange ideas with design students on how technology can become more useful, accessible and human-centred.
Psychology students can bring behavioural insights into conversations around management, communication, user experience and decision-making.
Communication design students can help shape how ideas are expressed, visualised and shared with the world.
Management students learn how design, data, communication and human behaviour influence real-world decisions.
These daily interactions teach students more than academics. They help students develop communication, confidence, adaptability, teamwork and leadership.
Designed Around Wellbeing and Sustainability
A Campus That Supports You and the Planet

WPU GŌA’s campus is also built around wellbeing and sustainability. The campus brings together residential spaces, dining spaces, sport and wellness areas, academic spaces, cultural zones, and community areas. The design provides a holistic student experience that integrates academic success with physical health, emotional well-being, social connection and personal development. The campus is also a showcase for an inspirational sustainability vision. Focused on water positivity, energy positivity, zero waste and biodiversity enhancement, sustainability is experienced by students not as a concept to be learned at school, but as part of their everyday campus life.
Living Learning Scholar Communities
Where shared spaces create stronger integrated thinkers.

A key part of the WPU GŌA experience is its Living Learning Scholar Communities. These communities are intended to create a lively academic and social community in which students are able to grow together.

Students are urged to ask questions, debate, create, and participate actively in campus life. They are not isolated within their own programmes but join a wider community built around curiosity, collaboration and shared growth.
A residential university in India should provide the right ambience for students to become independent, responsible and future-ready.
More Than Scale. A Stronger Student Experience.

Many students and parents search for India’s largest residential university or the largest residential university in India while comparing higher education options. But the true value of a residential campus is not measured by size alone.What counts is how the campus helps its students learn, live, and grow.

WPU GŌA is a fully residential environment, which supports students in their academic, social and personal growth. It gives them the opportunity to explore ideas, build friendships, learn discipline and prepare for a future that demands both depth and flexibility.
Know more
A Campus Built for the Future
The WPU GŌA campus is designed for students who want an immersive university experience.
Here, learning does not end when class ends. It continues in conversations, projects, communities, shared spaces, and everyday campus life.
WPU GŌA brings together residential learning, transdisciplinary education, sustainability, and student wellbeing to create a campus experience built for the future.
Frequently Asked Questions

Yes. WPU GŌA is designed as a fully residential university in India, where students live, learn, collaborate and grow within one connected campus environment. Residential life is not treated as separate from academics. It is part of the overall learning experience.

At WPU GŌA, students do not simply stay in hostels after class. The campus is designed as a living-learning ecosystem where academic spaces, residential spaces, dining spaces, sport, wellness, culture and community life work together. This helps students continue learning through conversations, projects, peer interaction and everyday campus experiences.

Living Learning Scholar Communities are academic and residential communities where students live and learn together. They are designed to encourage shared learning, reflection, collaboration and active participation in campus life. These communities help students grow not only within their own programme but also through interaction with students from other disciplines.

When students from different programmes live and learn together, collaboration becomes natural. Engineering students can work with design students, psychology students can bring behavioural insights, communication design students can shape how ideas are expressed, and management students can understand how people, systems, data and decisions connect. This daily exchange helps students become stronger, more integrated thinkers.

WPU GŌA offers future-ready undergraduate programmes across areas such as computer science and engineering, electronics and semiconductor engineering, energy systems engineering, integrated product design, communication design, human-computer interaction, economics, psychology, management, and film, media and communications.

The campus is designed to support academic growth along with physical health, emotional wellbeing, mental clarity and social connection. Residential life brings students closer to faculty mentors, peers, wellness spaces, sport, dining, student clubs and community experiences that help them build balance and resilience.

Yes. Sustainability is an important part of the WPU GŌA campus vision. The campus is designed around ideas such as water positivity, energy positivity, zero waste and biodiversity enhancement. Students experience sustainability not only as a subject of study but as part of everyday campus life.

Goa offers a unique setting where culture, nature, tourism, development and sustainability meet. This makes it a powerful learning environment for students. It allows them to study real-world challenges in a living context and understand how different systems are connected.

No. WPU GŌA is built around a holistic university experience. Along with academics, students are encouraged to participate in peer learning, student clubs, sport, cultural activities, wellness initiatives, community engagement and collaborative projects.

Living on campus helps students become more independent, responsible and collaborative. They learn to manage their time, build friendships, participate in communities, work across disciplines, and develop important life skills such as communication, leadership, adaptability and teamwork.

Many students and parents search for India’s largest residential university or the largest residential university in India while comparing options. WPU GŌA’s strength lies not only in its residential scale but in the quality of its fully residential learning environment, where campus life, academics, wellbeing, sustainability and collaboration come together.

Students can apply through the WPU GŌA admissions process on the official website. The process includes online registration, application submission, entrance exam where applicable, personal interaction, merit list, provisional offer, fee payment and document verification.

title: Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership
description: Learn how connected thinking across disciplines is shaping innovation, leadership, and future-ready problem solving.

blocks.text: The future belongs to those who can integrate knowledge across boundaries. The future will reward those who can connect disciplines, technologies, cultures, and ideas. The most significant opportunities of the coming decades will emerge not within narrow silos, but at the intersections of engineering and design, business and technology, psychology and data, law and innovation, health and life sciences, sustainability and enterprise.

At WPU Goa, we are building a university for this new era. We are intentionally designing a university where technology, creativity, human insight, systems thinking, and real-world impact converge to solve the most complex challenges of our time.

Our academic vision is rooted in transdisciplinary learning, global relevance, applied research, entrepreneurial thinking, and human-centred innovation. Students will be educated not only to succeed in existing careers, but to create new pathways in fields that are rapidly evolving.

Over the coming years, WPU Goa will continue to develop a future-facing portfolio of programmes across high-growth sectors. Each domain is designed to combine academic rigour with practical relevance, enabling graduates to become leaders, builders, innovators and changemakers in a connected world.
Engineering

Engineering is undergoing a profound transformation. Digital technologies, artificial intelligence, advanced manufacturing, semiconductor ecosystems, robotics, energy transitions, and intelligent infrastructure are reshaping industries worldwide. Tomorrow’s engineers must be fluent in both core principles and emerging technologies.

At WPU Goa, engineering and computing are not narrow technical fields, they are the engines of sustainable, intelligent, and human-centric innovation. Our programs fuse core engineering principles with artificial intelligence, robotics, high-performance computing, data analytics, and systems thinking to address real-world challenges in energy, healthcare, manufacturing, smart cities, and environmental stewardship.

At WPU Goa, engineering education will combine mathematics, scientific depth, computational capability, hands-on experimentation, and real-world problem solving. Students will work across domains, build prototypes, engage with industry challenges, and learn how to apply engineering intelligence to society’s most pressing needs—from smart systems and sustainable energy to biomedical innovation and autonomous technologies.

Students build rigorous technical mastery while developing the ability to design ethical, scalable, and future-proof solutions. Through project-based learning, industry collaborations, and transdisciplinary studios, they learn to bridge traditional engineering with emerging technologies such as digital twins, autonomous systems, and climate-resilient infrastructure. The focus is on creating engineers who are not only technically brilliant but also capable of leading cross-functional teams and driving meaningful global impact.
Indicative Programmes
B. Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering – AI & Machine Learning
B. Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering – Data Science & Analytics
B. Tech. in Computer Science & Engineering – Cybersecurity & Systems Design
B. Tech. in Electronics & Semiconductor Engineering – Semiconductor and Chip Design
B. Tech. in Electronics & Semiconductor Engineering – Embedded & IoT Systems
B. Tech. in Energy Systems Engineering – Renewable Energy and Climate Tech
B. Tech. in Energy Systems Engineering – Smart Grids & Energy Analytics
B. Tech. in Intelligent Systems – Robotics, Automation & Autonomous Systems
B.Tech. in Biomedical Engineering – Medical Devices & Diagnostics
Design & Human Experience

In the modern economy, design is strategy. It shapes products, interfaces, brands, environments, mobility systems, digital ecosystems, and the experiences through which people interact with the world. As automation grows, uniquely human strengths such as creativity, empathy, storytelling and aesthetic intelligence become even more valuable.

Design at WPU Goa is the art and science of shaping meaningful human experiences at the intersection of technology, sustainability, and culture. Our programs blend product innovation, interaction design, communication, immersive media, and systems thinking to create solutions that are beautiful, functional, responsible, and deeply user-centred.

Students work in collaborative studios alongside engineers, psychologists, business thinkers, and media professionals, turning bold ideas into tangible prototypes that address real societal and industrial needs from smart cities and sustainable mobility to immersive storytelling and human-AI interaction. The curriculum emphasises hands-on prototyping, ecological principles, industry-connected projects, and transdisciplinary collaboration from day one, producing “ready and able” designers equipped for the future job market. Graduates will be equipped to shape the next generation of consumer products, digital services, communication systems and immersive realities.
B. Des. in Integrated Product Design – Service & Systems Design
B. Des. in Integrated Product Design – Sustainable Design
B. Des. in Integrated Product Design – Interaction Design
B. Des. in Communication Design – Visual Communication & Identity Systems Design
B. Des. in Communication Design – Animation and VFX Design
B. Des. in Communication Design – Interaction Design
B. Des. in Interaction Design – User Experience & Interface Design
B. Des. in Interaction Design – Human–AI Interaction
B. Des. in Immersive Media Design – XR, Gaming & Virtual Worlds
Business Management and Entrepreneurship

Business leadership is being redefined by digital disruption, shifting global markets, sustainability priorities, new consumer behaviour and the rise of entrepreneurship. Organisations need leaders who can interpret data, understand people, create value responsibly, and innovate at speed.

We are reimagining business education for an era of exponential technological and societal change. Our programs fuse rigorous management training with entrepreneurship, digital innovation, sustainability, and ethical leadership creating purpose-driven leaders and venture builders who can navigate complexity while generating both economic and social value.

At WPU Goa, business education will combine management fundamentals with venture creation, technology fluency, global thinking, strategic communication and experiential learning. Students will engage with live projects, startup ecosystems, market simulations and cross-disciplinary collaboration with design, engineering and behavioural sciences.

Every pathway is deeply experiential and transdisciplinary, allowing students to move fluidly between strategy, technology, behavioural insights, and global impact. Specialisations integrate real-world applications such as AI strategy, sustainable enterprise, creator economies, and venture scaling, ensuring graduates are equipped to lead in dynamic industries while contributing to a more responsible and inclusive future.
Bachelor of Management Studies (Hons.) – Global Business & Startups
Bachelor of Management Studies (Hons.) – Digital Economy & E-Commerce
Bachelor of Management Studies (Hons.) – Sustainability & Enterprise
BBA in Business Enterprise Development (Hons.) – Innovation & Growth Strategy
MBA in Entrepreneurship & Venture Creation
MBA in AI Integration Strategy
MBA in Sustainable Tourism, Hospitality and Event Management
MBA in Finance & Investments
Humanities and Social Sciences

In an age of intelligent machines, human understanding becomes a strategic advantage. Psychology, economics, public policy, behavioural science, ethics and social analytics help explain how individuals think, communities function, institutions evolve and societies respond to change. True innovation begins with deep understanding of human behaviour, society, economics, and decision-making.

Our programs in psychology, economics, public policy, and social sciences equip students with analytical depth, ethical clarity, and behavioural insights needed to shape inclusive, evidence-based futures at the intersection of technology, policy, and human well-being.

By integrating quantitative methods, computational tools, philosophical inquiry, and real-world application, students learn to tackle complex societal challenges from behavioural economics and fintech to computational social science and public governance. The transdisciplinary approach connects these fields naturally with engineering, design, business, and law, producing graduates who can drive thoughtful, data-informed change.
Graduates will be prepared for careers in policy, consulting, research, organisational development, behavioural strategy, communication and public leadership.
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Psychology – Industrial Psychology, Organizational Behaviour & Consumer Psychology
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Economics – Quantitative & Computational Economics
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Economics – Development Economics & Public Policy
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Economics – Behavioural Economics & Decision Science
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Computational Social Science – Big Data & Society
B.A. (Hons.) in Philosophy, Politics & Economics (PPE)
Law, Policy & Governance

The legal profession is entering a new era shaped by artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, data privacy, global commerce, regulatory complexity and rapidly evolving public institutions. Tomorrow’s legal leaders must understand not only law, but technology, governance, economics and societal transformation.

Law at WPU Goa is future-oriented and deeply integrated with technology, ethics, sustainability, and global governance. Students explore how legal frameworks must evolve to address artificial intelligence, data privacy, cyber security, climate systems, and public policy preparing them to become thoughtful policymakers, tech-law experts, and ethical leaders.

At WPU Goa, legal education will integrate doctrinal strength with policy reasoning, negotiation, strategic thinking and contemporary regulatory understanding. Students will engage with case analysis, public affairs, innovation law and future governance challenges. We aim to prepare graduates for advocacy, corporate law, public service, policy leadership and emerging domains where law intersects with innovation.
B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) – Constitutional Law & Public Leadership
B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) – Corporate Law & Business Regulation
Specialisation Tracks – Technology Law / Cyber Law & Digital Rights / AI Governance & Ethics / Data Privacy & GDPR
Health, Environment & Sustainable Systems

We are entering an era where human health, environmental systems, and technological innovation are inextricably linked. The future will demand professionals who understand biology, technology, nutrition, diagnostics, wellness science and innovation ecosystems together. Our programs combine cutting-edge science with policy, design, and systems thinking to create holistic solutions for planetary and human well-being addressing climate change, public health, nutrition, wellness, and sustainable infrastructure.

Climate resilience, ecological responsibility and resource transformation will define economic and social progress in the coming decades. Every sector from finance and infrastructure to mobility and agriculture requires leaders who understand sustainability in both principle and execution.

Transdisciplinary collaboration with engineering, design, and business clusters ensures graduates can develop integrated approaches to ESG challenges, health-tech, and climate-resilient systems. Combining scientific foundations with entrepreneurial thinking and practical application, students will explore emerging opportunities in biomedical systems, health technology, longevity sciences, preventive wellness and life science innovation. Our graduates will be positioned to contribute to one of the world’s fastest growing and most socially important sectors.

WPU Goa’s sustainability ecosystem will integrate science, engineering, policy, enterprise and systems thinking. Students will study how to design low-carbon futures, responsible organisations, resilient infrastructure and practical climate solutions. We seek to prepare graduates who can lead meaningful change across business, government, technology and civil society.
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Nutrition, Wellness & Longevity
B.Tech. in Biomedical Engineering – Devices, Prosthetics & Diagnostics
B.Tech. in Biotechnology Engineering – Biotechnology Systems & Applied Biology
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Bioinformatics & Genomics
B.Tech. Environmental Engineering – Water, Waste & Circular Systems
B.Tech. Climate Systems Engineering – Coastal & Resilience Infrastructure
Bachelor of Management Studies (Hons.) – Sustainable Enterprise Leadership
MBA ESG Strategy & Responsible Business
Creative Media, Arts & Communication

The global economy increasingly rewards storytellers, creators, communicators and designers of memorable experiences. Digital media, content ecosystems, festivals, tourism, hospitality, entertainment and personal brands are reshaping how people engage with culture and commerce.

In the age of digital storytelling, creator economies, and visual culture, we are training the next generation of media professionals who blend artistic excellence with technological fluency, strategic thinking, and audience engagement. Our programs prepare students to shape culture, influence opinion, and build meaningful connections across traditional and emerging platforms.

Located in Goa, WPU Goa is uniquely positioned to build a distinctive centre of excellence in media, creativity and experience-led enterprise. Students will learn how to create narratives, build audiences, design experiences and lead brands across physical and digital worlds. Graduates will be prepared for careers in content, communication, destination strategy, hospitality innovation, media entrepreneurship and the creator economy.
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Film, Media & Communication – Content Creation & Storytelling
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Film, Media & Communication – Digital Branding & Audience Growth
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Hospitality & Experience Management – Luxury Service Leadership
B.Sc. (Hons.) in Experience Economy – Events, Festivals & Tourism Design
Bachelor in Cross Reality (XR) – Immersive Experiences & Media
A University in Motion

title: International Candidates
description: WPU GŌA is a fully residential campus that embraces a transdisciplinary philosophy of education. The WPU GŌA Admissions Committee looks beyond academic performance and seeks candidates who demonstrate strong character, meaningful experiences, and clear future motivation. Our admissions process is…

What do we look for in International students?

WPU GŌA is a fully residential campus that embraces a transdisciplinary philosophy of education. The WPU GŌA Admissions Committee looks beyond academic performance and seeks candidates who demonstrate strong character, meaningful experiences, and clear future motivation. Our admissions process is designed to identify well-rounded, high-potential students who can actively contribute to and enrich the learning environment at WPU GŌA.
Assessment Framework
The internal assessment process holistically evaluates the following components of the Candidate’s profile:
Past academic records
Online Personal Interaction
Extracurricular activities and/or professional achievements

At WPU GŌA, we evaluate candidates based on their holistic performance using the ABC Model across all evaluation parameters. Selection is not determined by a single score; instead, emphasis is placed on the overall profile, which should demonstrate strength and potential in various aspects. While higher scores can enhance selection prospects, we also consider consistent academic performance, the quality of responses in application essays, personal interactions, and meaningful extracurricular or professional achievements.
Admission Process Flow
Step 1: Create an Online Account on the website
Step 2: Fill the Registration Form (See “ How to register section ”)
Step 3: Appear for the Personal Interaction
Step 4: Check the Result (Dates/Results will be notified on the student dashboard)
Step 5: Receive Provisional Offer Letter upon Selection (Will be received on the registered email ID)
Step 6: Complete the payment of the Processing Fee and Offer Acceptance Fee
Step 7: Verification of the Payment and Documents by the Admissions Officer
Step 8: Receive Confirmation of verification with the student’s Unique Identification Number (Will be received on the registered email ID)
Step 9: Submit Original Documents ( Original Documents required for Physical Verification )
Step 10: Welcome to WPU GŌA
Programmes Offered
Undergraduate degrees are offered in the following majors:
Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech.)
Computer Science & Engineering
Electronics & Semiconductor Engineering
Energy Systems Engineering
Bachelor of Design (B.Des.)
Integrated Product Design
Communication Design
Human Computer Interaction (HCI)
Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) (Hons.)
Psychology
Economics
Film, Media, and Communications
Bachelor of Management Studies (BMS)
Selection and Evaluation Criteria

The Admissions Committee of WPU GŌA evaluates the complete profile of each candidate holistically to identify well-rounded, high-potential candidates who will thrive in, and actively contribute to, our residential learning community.

All candidates will be evaluated on their past academic performance and must meet programme-specific minimum requirements as detailed in the Eligibility Criteria . Candidates are required to submit a written essay (maximum of 750 words) and a video essay (2-3 minutes in length). Both the written and video essays will be assessed for authenticity, depth of reflection, and alignment with the ABC Model . B.Des. candidates are additionally required to submit a portfolio showcasing their creative work and design thinking capabilities.

The Personal Interaction provides an opportunity for the Admissions Committee to understand each candidate’s motivations, thought processes, and fit with the transdisciplinary philosophy of WPU GŌA. Supporting documents, including resumes, extracurricular certificates, and letters of recommendation, add value by providing deeper context about a candidate’s potential.
Programme-wise Eligibility Criteria
International Admission Categories
A. Foreign Nationals (FN): A candidate is eligible as a Foreign National if:
They hold citizenship and a valid passport of any country other than India.
Includes:
FN-SAARC Countries: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka
FN-Non-SAARC Countries
B. CIWGC / CIWSEA: Children of Indians working in:
Gulf Countries or
Southeast Asian Countries are eligible under this category (proof of parental employment mandatory).
C. OCI (Overseas Citizenship of India): A candidate is eligible under the OCI category if they have been granted OCI status and hold a valid OCI card .
D. NRI (Non-Resident Indian): A candidate is eligible under the NRI category if they are sponsored by an NRI who is an immediate or close relative . Eligible NRI sponsors include:
Father or Mother
Real Brother or Sister
Paternal Grandfather or Grandmother
Maternal Grandfather or Grandmother
Real Brother or Sister of the Father (Paternal Uncle/Aunt)
Real Brother or Sister of the Mother (Maternal Uncle/Aunt)
For International Candidates
Essential:
English Language Requirement: Medium of Instruction (MOI–English) or valid IELTS/TOEFL/PTE score.
Required Documents: Passport, academic transcripts, certificates, photographs, visa copy, medical fitness certificate, and proof of funds.
Note:

Candidates must have successfully completed the Class 12/10+2 examination (or equivalent) from a recognized board such as the International Baccalaureate (IB), Indian School Certificate (ISC), Cambridge International / CAIE, Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), or State Boards. Candidates with grades/GPA/credit points must submit an equivalence/conversion certificate from the respective examination board or the Association of Indian Universities (AIU)
For all Foreign Nationals, a valid passport is essential
A mandatory FRRO registration and an Indian visa are necessary. Our Admissions Team will assist you in obtaining these documents
* For B.Des. candidates, portfolio submission is mandatory

Each candidate is solely responsible for ensuring that they meet the prescribed eligibility criteria for the Undergraduate Programme at WPU GŌA. WPU GŌA reserves the right to verify eligibility at any stage of the admission process and to reject or cancel the candidature or admission of any candidate found to be ineligible. In such cases, the admission shall be deemed null and void, irrespective of the stage of the admission process or any prior communication of selection or admission
All International Baccalaureate (IB) students are required to have a minimum of 24 credit points with an awarded IB-Diploma.

Candidates from the IB Career-related Programme (IBCP) must have completed the two-year IBCP along with all mandatory core components and a recognised career-related study. For BMS, B.Sc. (Hons.) and B.Des. programmes, candidates must have two IB Diploma Programme (DP) subjects, with at least one subject at Higher Level (HL). For the B.Tech. programme, candidates must have three IB DP subjects, including Mathematics and relevant science subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Biology, or Computer Science, with at least one subject at Higher Level (HL). A minimum score of 3 points in each DP subject is mandatory
Cambridge International (CAIE) board awarded “A” Levels (12 equivalent)
An equivalence or conversion certificate from the respective board (IB-DP/ IB-CP/ ICSE/ CAIE)/ AIU is mandatory in cases where the results are issued in grades or credit points.
How to Register
Step 1: Pre-Registration
Candidates must register on the admission portal at the website:
Enter details in the prescribed format
Verify email address and mobile number
Select the International Category as per applicability
Step 2: Complete the Online Registration Form
Candidates MUST complete ALL sections of the online registration form
Section 1: Personal Details
Section 2: Area of Interest
Section 3: Academic Details
Section 4: Written Essay Section
Section 5: Video Essay
Section 6: Documents Upload
Section 7: Declaration and Submission
Enclosures with Registration
Candidates are advised to have all necessary documents ready before starting the online Registration process.
Mandatory Original Documents required for Physical Verification
10th Mark Sheet/ Certificate
12th/ Diploma Mark Sheet/ Certificate
National ID / Passport / Aadhar Card
Leaving/Transfer Certificate
Migration Certificate
Parent Passport Copy (If required by admissions committee)
Original Employer Certificate (Applicable for NRI & CIWGC/CIWSEA students)
Original Sponsorship Letter (Applicable for NRI & CIWGC/CIWSEA students)
NRI Certificate (Applicable for NRI & CIWGC/CIWSEA students)
OCI Certificate (Applicable for OCI students)
A mandatory FRRO registration and an Indian visa are necessary. Our Admissions Team will provide you with the guidance you need to procure these documents.
Any other document as prescribed by the admission team
Written Essay

WPU GŌA employs a holistic review process for potential students, focusing on more than just standardized test scores. The written essay allows candidates to highlight their individuality, goals, and values. This is an opportunity to share their unique experiences, challenges overcome, and leadership qualities, helping evaluators understand their potential for academic success and community contribution at WPU GŌA.
ABC Model of Selection
WPU GŌA places great importance on three interconnected dimensions (ABC) that reflect the skills and mindset essential for thriving in our transdisciplinary learning environment.
A – Agility & Empathic Responsiveness, which is defined by the ability to adapt to change, show resilience, and demonstrate empathy
B – Broad-Minded Learning Orientation & Self-Development, which is demonstrated by aspects such as curiosity-driven learning, independent thinking, and openness to growth
C – Curiosity for Complex Problems & Co-Creation for Change, which is exemplified by engagement with real-world issues through collaboration and teamwork
In writing the essay, the candidate should focus on these three dimensions, ensuring that they address how they might possess these attributes.
Guidance for Writing an Effective Essay
While not mandatory, a candidate might be guided in structuring the essay by addressing aspects such as:

Discussing how they navigated a challenge with agility & empathy by reflecting on a personal, academic, or social challenge that required them to adapt, persist, or support others. They could consider addressing some or all of the following:
What was the challenge, and why was it significant?
How did they respond? What actions did they take?
What did they learn about themselves or others through this experience?
How has this shaped their ability to handle uncertainty or complexity?
What skills would they like to develop further to become more adaptable and empathetic?

Explaining aspects of learning beyond the classroom through a description of activities or pursuits outside regular academic studies that have shaped their thinking or interests. They could consider addressing some or all of the following:
What activities have they engaged in? (sports, arts, projects, hobbies, entrepreneurial ventures, special interests, etc.)
Why did they choose to invest time in these areas?
How did these experiences broaden their perspective or challenge their assumptions?
What new questions, interests, or insights emerged?
How do they plan to continue or deepen this learning journey?

Discuss how they contributed to change through collaboration by reflecting on their involvement with a cause, community initiative, or collaborative project addressing a real-world issue. They could consider addressing some or all of the following:
Were they part of an initiative? (volunteering, sustainability efforts, social justice work, community organizations, candidate advocacy, etc.)
What drew them to this cause?
What makes this issue complex or challenging to address?
What did they learn from working with others, especially those with different perspectives or backgrounds?
How do they envision contributing to similar efforts in the future?

The above are just examples. A candidate is at liberty to write an essay that they believe best describes their unique background, perspective, and experiences, and how these place them at the very top of all candidates applying for admission to WPU GŌA.
It is important that candidates keep the following in mind:
The written essay section is a separate section in the registration form, and it should be written in English

The 750-word limit must be strictly adhered to. Shorter essays are permitted, but exceeding this limit will result in the essay not being evaluated, with the consequence of potentially not getting admitted to WPU GŌA
The essay should be authentic and express the writer’s genuine voice, perspectives, and lived experiences
It should reflect deeply on their journey, focusing on self-awareness and personal growth rather than listing achievements

It should be written honestly without exaggeration or falsification. There are no right or wrong answers; what matters is clarity of thought and how what is meaningful to them is conveyed to the committee
Video Essay

In addition to the written essay, candidates are required to submit a short video (2-3 minutes) in which they introduce themselves and explain why the committee should select them. It is an opportunity to build on the written essay, convince the committee of their level of preparation and ability to communicate, and position themselves effectively. Candidates should consider this as their “sales pitch”, answering the critical question of why they should be picked above other extremely well-qualified candidates for a very small number of spots in a unique environment that will not only shape their futures but also provide experiences, networks, and expertise that will ensure success professionally and in life.
The Video essays are to be submitted within the registration form as a link to a UNLISTED YouTube video in the provided space.
Personal Interaction
All candidates who submit the registration form will be invited to participate in an online personal interaction as part of the admission process.
Duration: 20 to 30 minutes.
Your personal interaction will include a discussion based on your overall profile.
Important Notes
The personal interaction is conversational in nature
There are no right or wrong answers; authenticity and honest reflection are valued
The interaction is designed to be a two-way dialogue where you may also ask questions about the programme
For B.Des. candidates, the Personal Interaction will also include a discussion on the submitted portfolio
Registration Form Status
IN PROGRESS
Candidates with their forms marked as ‘In Progress’ must complete all mandatory fields of the registration form.
ACCEPTED
This means that the admission team has acknowledged that your details are complete.
Decision Status
The status of your Registration will be communicated through the Registration Portal and/or registered email address. The possible statuses are as follows:
SELECTED
Candidates selected based on the prescribed selection and merit criteria are required to proceed with the subsequent admission steps, including:
A Provisional Offer is given to the candidate
Payment of the prescribed admission fees
Enrolment and online eligibility verification
Submission of original documents, as specified by the University
WAITLISTED
If you are placed on the Waiting List, please await further updates. Your Registration may be considered in future admission rounds, depending on seat availability.
REJECTED
Candidates marked as Rejected have not met the prescribed selection criteria for the applied programme and will not be considered for further stages of the admission process.
Candidates are advised to regularly check the Online Portal and their registered email for updates on their registration status and any further instructions from the Admissions Office
Seats will be allotted on a first-come, first-served basis
Fee Structure
Undergraduate Programme Fees for CIWGC-SEA, SAARC, FN, and OCI

The total programme fee is $10,000 per year for CIWGC-SEA, SAARC, FN, and OCI category students. However, for the inaugural batch ONLY, the annual fee would be $8,335. Additionally, ALL students applying in this category will receive a 50% scholarship on academic and student service fees

In addition to the above fee, a security deposit of $500 is to be paid at the time of payment of the Offer Acceptance Fee. The Security Deposit will be refunded upon graduation or withdrawal from the programme
A one-time Processing Fee of $300 is payable at the time of offer acceptance and is non-refundable. All other fees above are per annum
In the event that an international student is required to reside on campus during the winter or summer term, special permission shall be obtained, and an additional meal plan fee shall apply
The payment is to be made in USD only
Fee Structure for NRI
Undergraduate Programme Fees for NRI Category (in USD)
The actual total programme fee is $12,000 per year for the NRI Category. The above fee applies to the Inaugural Batch ONLY
International Scholarship
International Scholarship is offered to students from the CIWGC, CIWSEA, OCI, SAARC, and Foreign Nationals. An international scholarship cannot be clubbed with any other scholarship.
For all international candidates, a 50% domestic fee waiver will be awarded by WPU GŌA on the tuition fee in the form of a scholarship (except NRI Category).
This scholarship does not apply to the Accommodation & Campus Living Fee Components (i.e., Student Housing fee and Meal Plan)
All scholarships will be awarded on A FIRST-COME, FIRST-SERVED BASIS
Academic performance & attendance should be maintained throughout every year for the continuation of the scholarship
Fee Payment Schedule
First Year CIWGC-SEA, SAARC, FN and OCI Category
* Security Deposit (refundable) of $500 is to be paid at the time of the Offer Acceptance Fee payment.

Note : In cases where the admission offer is issued close to the commencement date, the candidate shall ensure payment of the Offer Acceptance Fee and the first instalment no later than one day before the commencement date.
First Year NRI Category
Security Deposit (refundable) of $500 is to be paid at the time of the Offer Acceptance Fee payment.
Refund Policy

WPU GŌA will consider requests for cancellation/ withdrawal of admission from candidates only if such a request is made pursuant to the regulations prescribed by the university. The regulations include the procedure for cancellation/ withdrawal of admission as well as the rules for refunds.
*Commencement refers to the first day on campus for programme orientation/coursework.
Admission Schedule
If you have any questions, please contact the admissions team.
Note : Submitting your registration form early may result in an earlier invitation to participate in the selection process, enabling timely communication of your selection status.

title: International Faculty Development Programme
description: In collaboration with Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (The

blocks.text: Teaching across boundaries: Transdisciplinary Learning and Teaching
Organised by WPU GŌA
In collaboration with Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (The Netherlands)
August 2026
Panjim, Goa, India
Preparing educators for complexity and transformative learning

Higher education is increasingly called upon to prepare graduates to address complex societal challenges such as climate change, public health, technological transformation, sustainable development, and social inequality. These challenges rarely fit neatly within the boundaries of a single discipline. Instead, they require collaboration across academic fields and meaningful engagement with stakeholders beyond the university.

Transdisciplinary Learning and Teaching (TDL) equips educators with the knowledge, skills, and pedagogical approaches needed to create learning environments where students integrate diverse perspectives, collaborate across disciplines, and work on real-world challenges.

This three-day international Faculty Development Programme introduces participants to the foundations of transdisciplinary learning and provides practical methods for integrating these approaches into teaching, curriculum design, research supervision, and community-engaged education.

Whether you are new to transdisciplinary education or seeking to strengthen existing teaching practices, this programme offers practical tools, international perspectives, and opportunities to learn alongside colleagues from diverse academic backgrounds.
This programme is designed for:
Faculty members from all disciplines (from early-career to senior faculty).
Learning outcomes
By the end of the programme, participants will be able to:
Understand the principles and foundations of Transdisciplinary Learning and Teaching
Appreciate the role of transdisciplinary approaches in addressing complex societal challenges
Design learning activities that encourage collaboration across disciplines
Facilitate collaborative and participatory learning processes
Reflect on opportunities for implementing TDL within their own institutional context
Core Learning Themes

Through an immersive, highly interactive programme, participants will explore the conceptual foundations, practical methods, and facilitation skills needed to design and lead transdisciplinary learning in higher education. Sessions combine theory, participatory exercises, case-based learning, reflection, and practical application to participants’ own teaching contexts.
Understanding complexity (epistemology and disciplinary perspectives)
Working across boundaries (participatory learning and stakeholder engagement)
Making sense of systems (systems thinking)
Creating change (innovation and transformation)
Learning from practice (case studies)
Improving practice (reflexivity and reflexive monitoring)
Programme Highlights
International faculty
Interactive workshops
Hands-on learning activities
Practical teaching tools and frameworks
Certificate of Participation
Collaborative group work
International and local case studies
Networking with educators from diverse institutions
The facilitators:
Prof. Barbara Regeer

Prof. Barbara Regeer is Professor of Transdisciplinary Research for Purpose-led Transformation and University Research Chair at the Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam. She is internationally recognised for her pioneering work in transdisciplinary research, sustainability transitions, knowledge co-creation, systems innovation, and transformative learning. Prof. Regeer has led numerous international research and education initiatives and has extensive experience designing educational programmes that bridge science and society
Dr. Emma Emily de Wit

Dr. Emma Emily de Wit is Head of the Transdisciplinary Research Centre at WPU GŌA and an independent researcher specialising in transdisciplinary research and education. With more than ten years of experience in teaching and facilitating transdisciplinary learning, she works at the intersection of higher education, sustainability, participatory research, and knowledge cocreation. She has designed and facilitated faculty development programmes and collaborates with universities and international partners to strengthen innovative approaches to teaching and learning.
About the collaboration
This Faculty Development Programme is jointly organised by WPU GŌA in collaboration with the Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam.

The programme combines international expertise with practical experience in transdisciplinary education, offering participants an opportunity to learn from leading practitioners while engaging with innovative teaching methods that can be directly applied within their own institutions.

title: MoUs and Strategic Partnerships
description: At WPU GŌA, MoUs and strategic partnerships are built to take transdisciplinary learning beyond the classroom, beyond a single discipline and beyond traditional modes of education. These collaborations bring together academic, industry, research and institutional partners to generate new…

blocks.text: At WPU GŌA, MoUs and strategic partnerships are built to take transdisciplinary learning beyond the classroom, beyond a single discipline and beyond traditional modes of education. These collaborations bring together academic, industry, research and institutional partners to generate new opportunities for hands-on learning, innovation, mentoring, internships and real-world problem-solving. The purpose is to help students see the links between ideas from different subjects, learn from a wide range of experts and use knowledge in ways that make a difference. With each partnership, WPU GŌA aims to develop confident learners who are ready for the future, able to think for themselves, work together and create solutions that have a real impact on the world.
WPU GŌA Signs MoU with Makers Asylum Foundation to Advance Innovation and Maker-Led Learning

title: Our logo
description: The WPU GŌA symbol is rooted in a simple belief: education must serve humanity. The circle represents unity, balance, and continuity of learning. Inspired by the Ensō, it reflects mindfulness, wholeness, and the idea of becoming—never finished, always evolving. Blue stands for trust and global…

blocks.text: The WPU GŌA symbol is rooted in a simple belief: education must serve humanity. The circle represents unity, balance, and continuity of learning. Inspired by the Ensō, it reflects mindfulness, wholeness, and the idea of becoming—never finished, always evolving. Blue stands for trust and global perspective.
Maroon reflects courage, leadership, and purposeful action. Together, they form the Circle of Peace: A symbol that reflects what we believe, how we learn, and who we aim to become.
Globally informed.
Locally rooted.
Our Vision
Our Mission
Ōur Vision

“To be the world’s leading transdisciplinary university dedicated to peace and sustainability, designing and demonstrating regenerative futures, where human societies thrive in harmony within planetary boundaries.”
Ōur Mission

WPU GŌA exists to prepare leaders who think deeply, connect ideas across domains, and act with empathy. We educate leaders who can see the whole system, understand people, and design solutions with compassion. We generate research that pushes boundaries and enables real change, and we work to catalyse innovation and policy that shape the world we want to live in.

title: Privacy Policy
description: Read WPU Goa privacy policy outlining data collection, usage, cookies, and measures to protect user information and ensure secure access.

blocks.text: Thank you for visiting the official WPU GŌA site and taking a moment to read our Privacy Policy.

Your trust matters to us, and we take your privacy seriously. This policy explains how data is collected, stored, and used whenever you interact with our site. In brief, we gather personal details only when you choose to provide them. We never sell, trade, or rent your information to third parties.
All data is used exclusively for legitimate university activities and managed with strict care. For specifics on technical logs and voluntary submissions, please see the sections that follow.
Non-Personal Information That We Record
When you browse our site without submitting personal details, our servers automatically capture certain technical metadata about your session, including:
the Internet domain of your service provider;
the make and version of the web browser employed;
the operating system running on your device;
the date and time of each visit;
the specific pages accessed and the duration of each view; and
web address that referred you here, when relevant.

We process this information exclusively for statistical reporting and to refine site architecture, user flow, and overall performance. All data is reviewed only in aggregate form and is never used to identify individual visitors.
Cookies

WPU GŌA employs cookies on selected pages to deliver a smoother, more secure browsing experience. A cookie is a small text file placed on your device while you navigate a site; it helps the system recognise your session, preserve preferences, and enable interactive features. Cookies set by WPU GŌA do not capture personally identifiable information. Their sole purpose is to improve usability and streamline navigation.

You remain in control: most browsers let you receive a prompt before a cookie is stored or to block cookies altogether. Please be aware that disabling cookies may limit certain site functions. When using a shared or public computer, we recommend closing the browser once your session ends to protect your privacy.
Information Collected Through Interactive Forms
Specific sections of the WPU GŌA website contain secure, interactive forms through which you may choose to share personal details—for example, when you:
Submit an admissions enquiry,
Schedule a counselling session or campus tour,
Download a brochure or application,
Register for events, workshops, seminars, or tests, or
Send us an electronic mail.

Any data you supply, such as your name, email address, phone number, or programme interests, is recorded so we can answer your query, process your request, or keep you informed about relevant university matters. We collect such information only when you actively provide it, and you are free to explore the site anonymously if you wish.

Information received through these forms is used strictly for the purpose stated at the point of collection. It is never disclosed to unauthorised third parties and is retained only for the time needed to meet administrative or communication requirements consistent with applicable law and university policy.
Communication and Consent

If you submit your personal information and contact details to WPU GŌA, you expressly authorise WPU GŌA to contact you through telephone calls, SMS, email, or WhatsApp regarding admissions, academic programs, campus updates, services, application processes, and other university-related information.

This authorisation shall remain valid even if your mobile number is registered under the Do Not Disturb (DND) or National Customer Preference Register (NCPR). By voluntarily submitting your contact details through any form or electronic communication on our website, you provide explicit consent to receive such communication from WPU GŌA.
Such communication shall be considered consent-based communication initiated through your voluntary interaction with the university.
Email Communication

Email communication over the internet may not be fully secure against interception. If you include personally identifiable information in your email correspondence, such information will be used solely for responding to your request. Users are advised to provide only the information necessary for processing their enquiry.
Data Security

WPU GŌA implements reasonable administrative, technical, and procedural safeguards to protect personal information against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. While we strive to maintain security standards, no method of internet transmission is completely secure, and users share information at their own discretion.
Links to External Websites

The WPU GŌA website may contain links to external websites for additional information or resources. Once you leave our website, you are subject to the privacy policies of those external sites. WPU GŌA does not control and is not responsible for the content, practices, or policies of external websites.
Users are encouraged to review the privacy statements of any website where personal information is shared.
Changes to the Privacy Policy

WPU GŌA reserves the right to modify or update this Privacy Policy at any time without prior notice. Any changes will be reflected on this page. Continued use of the website after such modifications indicates acceptance of the updated policy.
For any clarification regarding this Privacy Policy, users may contact WPU GŌA through the official contact information available on the website.

blocks.text: Where Ideas Evolve and Innovation Takes Shape

At WPU GŌA, we conduct research with the aim for positive transformation. Our collaborative projects are focused on designing and evaluating innovations that lead to e.g. more sustainability, better health, and improved ways of living.
